ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
OF THE
COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
PASSED AT THE REGULAR SESSION OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY ,
WHICH WAS BEGUN IN THE CITY OF FRANKFORT , KENTUCKY ,
ON TUESDAY , JANUARY THE SIXTH , 1948 , AND ENDED FRIDAY,
MARCH THE NINETEENTH , 1948 .
CHAPTER 1
(H. B. 2 )
AN ACT to amend Section 117.155 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes,
relating to registration of voters .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 117.155 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is
amended to read as follows :
Notwithstanding the provisions of KRS 117.050 , the
judges of election in each precinct excepting those precincts
within the limits of a city of the first class , shall register each
qualified voter who makes application to them for registration
and who satisfactorily establishes his right to be registered .
The manner of registering shall conform to that provided by
law for registration by the county clerk. No person registered
on the day of a primary election shall be permitted to vote in
such election , nor in any election to be held within thirty (30)
days thereafter . The judges of election shall deliver the new
registrations to the county clerk immediately following the
primary election , and he shall enter them in the regular regis-
tration books .
Approved February 27, 1948 .
CHAPTER 2
(H. B. 5)
AN ACT appropriating money for the operation , maintenance , support
and functioning of the State Government of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky , including each of the various offices , departments , boards ,
entries.citation: Acts of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky, 1948, p. 16

classification.title: AN ACT to amend Section 117.155 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes, relating to registration of voters.
classification.summary: This act amends KRS 117.155 to allow judges of election in precincts, except those within cities of the first class, to register qualified voters who apply and establish their right to be registered. The registration process should follow that of the county clerk. Persons registered on the day of a primary election cannot vote in that election or within 30 days thereafter. New registrations are to be delivered to the county clerk after the primary election for entry into the regular registration books.
classification.reasoning: The statute pertains to voter registration procedures in Kentucky. While it mentions an exception for precincts within a city of the first class, there is no explicit or implicit mention of race or any discriminatory practice based on race. The provisions seem to apply generally to all qualified voters. Therefore, it is classified as 'no' for Jim Crow laws. The confidence is high because the text is clear and does not suggest racial discrimination. The category is 'voting' as it directly relates to the process of voter registration. The document type is 'session_laws' as it is an Act passed by the General Assembly. No human review is needed.

providing for certain appropriations to be limited to specific pur-
poses and barring the use of appropriations for certain purposes ; re-
pealing all blanket and continuing appropriations not provided for
in this Act and appropriations made by any previous Act or Acts
of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , and

Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-

There is hereby appropriated out of the General Ex-

Withdrawal from the said Revolving , Trust and Agency Fund
by each of the said professional boards , or other boards ,

State Government may be made when and if needed for

and Agency Fund of such professional board or other board ,

classification.title: An Act relating to the Kentucky Building Commission
classification.summary: This act establishes and outlines the powers and duties of the Kentucky Building Commission. The commission is composed of the Governor, Lieutenant Governor, Attorney General, Commissioner of Finance, and Commissioner of Revenue. Its responsibilities include determining the need for and overseeing the construction, reconstruction, and equipping of state buildings and facilities, including utility services like roads and sewers. The commission is authorized to plan, advertise for bids, award contracts, and supervise construction, as well as employ necessary personnel and adopt rules and regulations. Expenditures are to be made in accordance with specific KRS chapters, and warrants issued by the Commissioner of Finance.

The text focuses on administrative and financial procedures related to construction and infrastructure. There is no mention of race, segregation, or any discriminatory practices. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law.

AN ACT relating to juries .
CHAPTER 3
(H. B. 9 )
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
1. Section 29.130 of the 1946 edition of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) At each term of circuit court, of circuit courts
having terms , the judge shall , in open court , draw from the
drum a sufficient number of names to procure the names of
twenty -four persons qualified to act as grand jurors , record
the names of the twenty-four qualified upon paper , certify
and sign it . The judge shall then , after having locked and
revolved or shaken the drum , reopen it and draw therefrom
the names of not less than thirty nor more than thirty - six
persons , in the discretion of the judge , to act as petit jurors ,
record their names on paper , certify and sign it. Sessions of
a court having continuous session held at the county seat of
a county in which there is a city of the second class at which
part of its sessions are authorized to be held shall be deemed
terms of court within the meaning of this section .
(2 ) In courts of continuous session , except the courts
in counties containing a city of the first class , the judge of
the court, or if the court has branches the judge of the
criminal branch , shall, in open court , on a day one week next
before the day upon which they shall be summoned to appear ,
draw from the drum a sufficient number of names to procure
twenty -four persons qualified to act as grand jurors , record
the names of the twenty -four qualified upon paper , certify
and sign it . In such courts the judge of the court , or if the
court has branches the judge of each division requiring the
services of a petit jury , shall , in open court , every four weeks ,
or oftener in the discretion of the judge , on a day one week
next before the day upon which they shall be summoned to
appear , draw from the drum the names of not less than thirty
nor more than thirty -six persons to act as petit jurors , record
their names on paper, certify and sign it . A petit jury drawn

(3 ) In courts of counties containing a city of the first
class , the judge of the criminal branch shall, on a day one
week next before the day on which they shall be summoned
to appear , draw from the drum a sufficient number of names ,
in the discretion of the judge , to procure a sufficient number
of persons qualified to act as grand jurors , and shall make
a written record thereof, which he shall certify and sign . In
such courts the judge of each division requiring the services
of a petit jury shall, every two weeks , on a day one week
next before the day upon which they shall be summoned to
appear , draw from the drum a sufficient number of names ,
not to exceed sixty , in the discretion of the judge , to procure
twenty -four persons qualified to act as petit jurors . The clerk
of the court or the official stenographer , as directed by the
court , shall prepare a record thereof on paper , which shall
be certified and signed by the judge .
(4 ) In courts of continuous session , if the day on which
the drawing is prescribed to be done is a holiday , the judge
shall draw the names on the following day.
( 5 ) The judge shall destroy the slips on which are
written the names placed on the lists as soon as the names
are recorded on the lists . If in drawing the grand jurors the
name of a person not having the qualifications of a grand
juror is drawn , it shall be forthwith returned to the drum ,
and if at any drawing the name of a person temporarily dis-
qualified is drawn , it shall be replaced in the drum, but if the
name of any person permanently disqualified for any jury
service is drawn , the slip on which it is written shall be
destroyed .
(6 ) From the list of names of persons qualified to act
as grand jurors , the next grand jury shall be drawn , and from
the list of names of persons qualified to act as petit jurors ,
not less than twenty -four nor more than thirty jurors shall
be selected , in the discretion of the judge , to constitute the
regular panel of the next petit jury .
Approved March 25 , 1948 .

CHAPTER 4
(H. B. 16 )
AN ACT relating to the special education of physically or mentally
handicapped children ; creating a Division of Special Education for
Handicapped Children ; providing for special educational facilities ;
and appropriating money therefor .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. As used in this Act :
(1 ) " Physically handicapped children " means children
of sound mind between the ages of 3 and 21 years who suffer
from any disability making it impracticable or impossible for
them to benefit from or participate in the normal classroom
program of the public schools in the school district in which
they reside and whose education requires a modification of
the normal classroom program .
( 2) " Educable mentally handicapped children " means
children between the ages of 5 and 21 years who because of
retarded mental growth ( as determined by recognized stan-
dardized tests ) are incapable of being educated profitably
and efficiently through ordinary classroom instruction but
who may be expected to benefit from special educational
facilities designed to make them economically useful and
socially adjusted .
(3) " Special education facilities " means special
schools , special classes , and special instruction . All special
education facilities shall be under the supervision of the
Superintendent of Public Instruction .
Section 2. The State Board of Education shall create
a "Division of Special Education for Handicapped Children ,
within the Bureau of Instruction in the State Department of
Education separate from any other existing division , to
supervise and direct a state program of special education for
physically and educable mentally handicapped children of
this Commonwealth . It shall be the duty of this division to
register all such children , to classify types of physically and
mentally handicapped children who may receive school in-
struction together , to recommend rules and regulations for

puted , reported , and approved , but in no event to exceed the
sum of $ 275 per annum for each physically handicapped child ,
or $ 125 per annum for each educable mentally handicapped
child . The State Treasurer shall pay such warrants out of
funds in the State Treasury that have been appropriated for
the purpose.
(4) No school district may be reimbursed under this Act
for costs for which financial aid is paid under any other Act .
Section 12. There is hereby appropriated to the Divi-
sion of Special Education , out of the General Expenditure
Fund in the State Treasury , as of the effective date of this
Act , for the purposes of administering this Act and reimburs-
ing local school districts as provided in this Act, the sum of
$ 70,000 , which shall be available for expenditure during the
balance of the 1947-1948 fiscal year and during the fiscal
years 1948-1949 and 1949-1950 . Any unexepended or unencum-
bered balance remaining of such appropriation at the end of
either of such fiscal years shall not lapse to the General Ex-
penditure Fund , but shall be available for expenditure during
the following fiscal year or years .
Section 13. Section 159.060 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is repealed .
Section 14. WHEREAS the lack of adequate educa-
tional facilities for handicapped children threatens to impair
their development as useful citizens of this Commonwealth ,
an emergency is declared to exist and this Act shall be in full
force and effect immediately upon its passage and approval .
Approved March 25 , 1948 .

AN ACT relating to the condemnation of the right to use or to make and
use underground shafts , slopes , passageways or entries , for the pur-
pose of operating coal mines or other mines and marketing the
products therefrom ; including provisions for immediate possession .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Whenever it is necessary for the owner of land to
have access over , under or through the land of one or more
other persons , for the purpose of operating a coal mine or
other mine , and marketing the products therefrom , and access
over the surface of the land of such other persons , for the
purpose , would be impracticable , but there exist shafts , slopes ,
passageways or entries below the surface of the land of such
other persons , suitable for the purpose and which could be
used for the purpose without undue or unreasonable incon-
venience to the owner thereof , or there exist coal seams or
other strata below the surface of the land of such other per-
sons , through which shafts , slopes , passageways or entries
may be made without unreasonable inconvenience to the
owner thereof, the right to use , or to make and use , such
shafts , slopes , passageways or entries may be condemned
under the procedure set forth in KRS 381.580 to 381.630 , sub-
ject to the exceptions prescribed in Section 2 of this Act . The
right of use so condemned shall include the right to install
tracks , power lines , lights , ventilating equipment , and other
things necessary for satisfactory use .
And provided further that in any case where the rights or
uses herein provided for are sought to be condemned through
any land already devoted to mining purposes , if such pro-
posed rights or uses in any wise interfere with or disrupt any
approved mining methods , plans or practices , as established
or projected as a part of a general plan of mining to conform
to or comply with any safety provisions , rules or regulations
under the existing mining laws of the State of Kentucky or of
the United States of America as now in effect or hereafter
enacted , such rights or uses shall be conclusively presumed

CHAPTER 6
(H. B. 30)
AN ACT relating to the practice of dental hygiene , amending and sup-
plementing the present statutes relating thereto .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Subsection ( 3 ) of Section 313.010 of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes is amended to read as follows :
( 3 ) " Dental hygiene " shall mean the treatment of hu-
man teeth by removing therefrom calcareous deposits , and
by removing accumulated accretion from directly beneath the
free margin of the gums , and by polishing the exposed surface
of the teeth , and shall also mean the making of x-ray expos-
ures of teeth and surrounding tissues ; Provided that nothing
in KRS 313.260 to 313.350 shall be so construed as to affect
the practice of medicine or dentistry nor to prevent students
of a dental college or university from practicing dental hy-
giene under supervision of their instructors .
§ 2. Section 313.290 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is
amended to read as follows :
Any person of good moral character over nineteen years.
of age who is a citizen of the United States of America , a
graduate of an accredited high school , and has successfully
completed two years of training in a school of dental hygiene
that is approved by the board , shall be eligible to take the
examination for the practice of dental hygiene .

CHAPTER 15
(H. B. 64 )
AN ACT creating a Legislative Research Commission , defining its powers
and duties , making an appropriation therefor , and repealing all laws
or parts of laws in conflict herewith .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. There is hereby created a Legislative Re-
search Commission to be composed of the Governor , the
President Pro Tem of the Senate , the Speaker of the House of
Representatives and the majority and minority Floor Lead-
ers of the Senate and House of Representatives . The Gover-
nor shall be ex officio chairman of the Commission , but may ,
for such periods as he may determine , designate the Lieuten-
ant Governor as a member of the Commission to act as chair-
man in his place . Any vacancy in the Commission shall be
filled by the remaining members thereof , provided , if the
vacancy is in the membership of the House of Representatives
then the successor shall be from the House , and if the vacancy
is from the Senate membership of the Commission then the
successor shall be from the Senate . A member thus elected to
fill any vacancy shall hold office for the unexpired term of his
predecessor .
( a ) The Legislative Research Commission shall meet
during regular and special sessions of the General Assembly

CHAPTER 16
(H. B. 65 )
AN ACT relating to the common school equalization fund , with particu-
lar reference to eligibility of school districts to participate in the
fund .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Subsection ( 1 ) of Section 157.053 of the Kentucky Re-
vised Statutes is amended to read as follows :
(1 ) Any board of education that has had its budgets and
salary schedule for the ensuing school year approved by the
State Board of Education , whose ratio of assessed valuation
of property to fair cash value is equal to the average ratio
throughout the state , as certified to the State Board of Edu-
cation by the Kentucky Tax Commission , and has levied for
school purposes a tax at the maximum rate permitted by law ,
shall have the privilege of applying for aid from the equaliza-
tion fund .
This Act shall take effect on July 1 , 1949 .
Approved March 24 , 1948 .

(H. B. 67)

relating to optometrists and the practice of optometry .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 311.210 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is
amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) The board may suspend , revoke or refuse to issue
a license to practice optometry for any of the grounds listed
in KRS 311.110 .

(3 ) Any person who violates this section shall be
punished by fine or imprisonment , or both , in the discretion
of the jury .
Section 2. That Section 437.120 , Kentucky Revised
Statutes be repealed , amended and re -enacted so that same ,
when re-enacted shall read as follows :
If any injury results to any person or property , by reason
of any act prohibited by subsection ( 2 ) of KRS 437.110 , the
person engaged , participating , aiding or abetting in such an
act shall be punished by fine or imprisonment , or both , in the
discretion of the jury , unless death results , in which case the
penalty for such offense shall be as now prescribed by law .
It shall be no mitigation of the offense under this section that
the offender acted through heat or passion or without malice ,
and the court trying the case shall so instruct the jury in
writing . The persons so confederating or banding together
shall be individually and collectively liable in damages , actual
and punitive , to the owner of property so damaged or de-
stroyed .
Approved March 12 , 1948 .

classification.summary: This statute outlines deductions and exemptions for income tax purposes in Kentucky. It specifies that the estimated annual rental value of owner-occupied residence property is not taxable income. CHAPTER 25
(H. B. 84 )
AN ACT to amend Section 63.080 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes ,
relating to the power of the Governor to remove officers appointed
by him .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 63.080 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is
amended to read as follows :
(1) Except as provided in subsection ( 2 ) of this section.
and otherwise provided by law , any person appointed by the
Governor , either with or without the advice and consent of
the Senate , may be removed from office by the Governor for
any cause the Governor deems sufficient , by an order of the
Governor entered in the Executive Journal removing the
officer .
( 2 ) Members of the Board of Trustees of the Univer-
sity of Kentucky , members of the Board of Regents respect-
ively of Eastern Kentucky State Teachers College , Western
Kentucky State Teachers College , Morehead State Teachers
College and Murray State Teachers College may not be re-
moved except for cause .
Approved March 25 , 1948 .

Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. No contestant under eighteen years of age shall be
issued or cause to be issued a license or allowed to participate
in any professional boxing or wrestling match or exhibition ,
nor shall any person obtain , or cause to be obtained , a license
to any person under eighteen years of age , to participate in
any professional boxing or wrestling match or exhibition .

Section 1 of this Act shall be fined not less than one hundred
dollars , nor more than five hundred dollars , and shall be
restrained for one year after the offense is committed from
participating in any boxing or wrestling match or exhibition .
Not Approved .

CHAPTER 30
(H. B. 126 )
AN ACT to provide a lien on radios , phonographs , and combinations
thereof , automatic music players , refrigerators and all portable
electrical instruments or appliances , in favor of the person, firm or
corporation engaged in the business of repairing , and repairing such
articles , and for a sale of such articles upon failure of the owner to
pay the charges for repairs , storage and expenses incidental to sale .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
(1 ) All persons , firms and corporations engaged in the
business of repairing radios , phonographs , combinations
thereof, automatic music instruments, refrigerators , and any
and all portable electrical instruments or appliances shall
have a lien upon such articles and appliances for the agreed
or reasonable charge for parts , supplies , accessories and
labor performed in their repair , said lien to attach upon
completion of such repairs .
( 2 ) After completion of repairs the owner shall be
notified thereof by registered mail , and if the repaired article
be not removed and all charges paid within 30 days after
giving the notice , the person , firm or corporation making
such repairs may hold such repaired articles and sell same
at public sale to pay all agreed or reasonable charges , includ-
ing charges for storage after notice of completion of repairs ,
and expenses incidental to advertisement and sale , accounting
to the owner for any excess of charges and expenses .

section (1 ) of this section shall be considered and estimated
in fixing the valuation of corporate franchises .
(3) Property shall be assessed for taxation at its fair
cash value , estimated at the price it would bring at a fair
voluntary sale .
(4) The situs of intangible personal property for pur-
poses of taxation shall be at the residence of the real or
beneficial owner , and not at the residence of the fiduciary or
agent having custody or possession . Any intangible property
owned by a resident shall be taxable in this state , unless by
the date of assessment he has changed his place of abode to
a place without this state with the bona fide intention of
continuing actually to abide permanently without this state .
The fact that a person again abides within this state within
six months from so changing his actual place of abode shall
be prima facie evidence that he did not intend permanently
to have his actual place of abode without this state . A person
so changing his actual place of abode and not intending per-
manently to continue it without this state and not having
listed his property for taxation as a resident of this state
shall , for the purpose of having his property assessed for
taxation within this state , be deemed to have resided , on the
day when his property should have been so assessed , at his
last actual or habitual place of abode within this state . The
fact that a person does not claim or exercise the right to vote
at public elections within this state shall not of itself consti-
tute him a nonresident of this state .
( 5 ) An administrator , executor , trustee , committee ,
curator or agent residing in this state shall not be liable for
taxes on intangible personal property held by him if the real
or beneficial owner of the property resides outside of this

CHAPTER 35 81
Statutes Section 137.190 . This Act shall be known as the Cooper-
Howard -Hilton Act.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. An excise tax is imposed on every person ,
corporation or association which operates a running or trot-
ting horse race track at which betting is conducted through a
pari -mutuel or other similar system . The tax shall be an
amount equal to three per cent of the total amount of all
money deposited in pari -mutuel pools or similar pools (in-
cluding any pool , such as a daily double pool , even though
tickets sold for such a pool are not vended by a mechanical
or electrical device nor the totals of such a pool computed and
determined by a totalizator ) .
Section 2. The tax imposed by section 1 of this Act shall
not apply to an association or corporation which operates a
running or trotting horse race track which is not authorized
by its charter to pay dividends ( currently or in liquidation )
to any of its stockholders , or to make any distribution of its
profits to its members , and all the profits of such association
or corporation , in excess of operating expenses and amounts
expended or reasonably reserved for the repair , maintenance
or improvement of the track , grounds and facilities at which
it so operates , are used solely for, or are paid to any other
association , corporation , institution , agency , foundation ,
society or other organization in the United States which is
exempt from Federal income taxes , to be used by it or another
such organization solely for scientific , educational or chari-
table purposes in such a manner that no part of such profits
inure to the benefit of any stockholder or member of such
organization nor to the benefit of any stockholder or member
of the corporation or association which operates the track .
But no association or corporation shall be entitled to the
exemption granted in this section if any officer , employee ,
member or shareholder thereof shall receive or be entitled to
receive , directly or indirectly , any pecuniary profit therefrom
except reasonable compensation for necessary services
actually rendered in the ordinary conduct of its business .
entries.citation: Acts of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky, 1948, p. 96

classification.title: Cooper-Howard-Hilton Act
classification.summary: This act imposes an excise tax of three percent on the total amount of money deposited in pari-mutuel pools at running or trotting horse race tracks. It provides an exemption for associations or corporations that do not pay dividends or distribute profits to stockholders or members, and whose profits are used solely for scientific, educational, or charitable purposes by tax-exempt organizations. However, no officer, employee, member, or shareholder can receive any pecuniary profit except reasonable compensation for services rendered.
classification.reasoning: The statute imposes an excise tax on horse racing operations and outlines conditions for an exemption based on profit distribution and charitable use. There is no mention of race, nor any indication of discriminatory practices based on race. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law.

The Supervisor of pari -mutuel betting appointed by the

representative shall , at all reasonable times , have access to all
books , records , issuing or vending machines , adding machines
and all other pari -mutuel equipment for the purpose of exam-
ining and checking the same and ascertaining whether or not
the proper amount or amounts due the State are being or
have been paid.
Every person , Corporation or association required to

records so as to clearly show by a separate record the total
amount of money contributed to every pari -mutuel pool , in-
cluding daily double pools , if any .

CHAPTER 38
(H. B. 160 )
AN ACT relating to game and fish ; amending KRS 150.445 and subsec-
tion ( 2 ) of KRS 150.450 .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
1. Section 150.445 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) No person shall take fish by the use of a trotline ,
hand line , set line , throw line , or snag line than is longer than
the width of the stream in which it is used at the point where
such line is used .
(2 ) No person shall, within seven hundred yards of the
Kentucky Dam on the Tennessee River at Gilbertsville , unless
a shorter distance is prescribed by the division , nor within
two hundred yards of any other lock and dam on a navigable
stream , take fish other than by pole and line in hand , or place
or use any trotline , hand line , set line , throw line or snag line .
§ 2. Subsection ( 2 ) of 150.450 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
( 2 ) Any person may take by the use of licensed seines ,
the mesh of which is not less than two inches square , or by the
use of licensed hoop nets without wings , or with wings in the
Mississippi River only , any fish from the navigable streams
of this state . But no person shall take any fish by seines or
nets or place any seine or nets in any of these streams within
two hundred yards of the mouth of any stream entering into
those waters , nor within two hundred yards of the mouth of
any navigable stream within the state , nor nearer than seven
hundred yards below Kentucky Dam on the Tennessee River

CHAPTER 39
( H. B. 163 )
AN ACT providing for an advancement against compensation and ex-
penses to county tax commissioners .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is
created , to be numbered KRS 132.645 , and to read as follows :
The county tax commissioner of each county , except
counties containing a city of the first or second class , is en-
titled to receive an advancement by the state to defray neces-
sary official expenses and partial payment of the salaries of
himself and his deputies , which advancement shall be based
on fifty percent of the previous year's assessment and shall
be payable in six equal payments . Beginning on the first day
of July in each year , on the first day of each calendar month
for six consecutive months , the Department of Finance shall
authorize payment of the amount due for that month to the
county tax commissioner of each of said counties . The sum of
these advancements shall be deducted from the eighty percent
authorized to be paid the county tax commissioner by KRS
132.590 , Subsection ( 1 ) . If the office of county tax commis-
sioner becomes vacant , the sums advanced hereunder shall be
deducted from the yearly settlement when it is made .
Approved March 25 , 1948 .

stenographer for police court in cities of first class ; fixing the salary

The General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky hereby re-
peals Section 28.510 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes, effective as
of June 1, 1948 and re - enacts in lieu thereof the following , viz .:
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
―Section 28.510- STENOGRAPHER FOR POLICE
COURT IN CITIES OF THE FIRST CLASS . The judge of
the police court in cities of the first class may appoint , for a
term of not more than four (4) years , a stenographic re-
porter . He shall be paid by the city a salary of three thousand
($ 3,000.00 ) dollars per annum , and the judge may remove
him at pleasure . The stenographer may have such vacation
as the judge allows , not exceeding thirty ( 30 ) days in any one
year. During vacation , the judge of the police court may ap-
point a reporter pro tem who shall be paid at the rate of ten
( $ 10.00 ) dollars per diem during the vacation period of the
regular stenographic reporter . The reporter pro tem's salary
shall not exceed three hundred ($ 300.00 ) dollars in any one
year.

CHAPTER 42
(H. B. 171 )
AN ACT amending Section 402.080 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes,
relating to marriage licenses . It shall be known and cited as the
Mary E. Burnett Act.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 402.080 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is
amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) No marriage shall be solemnized without a license
therefor . The license shall be issued by the clerk of the
county in which the female resides at the time , unless the
female is of full age or a widow , and the license is issued on
her application in person or by writing signed by her , in
which case it may be issued by any county clerk .
( 2 ) No county clerk shall issue a marriage license until
the application for a marriage license remains on file , open to
the public , in the office of the county court clerk, for three
days before license is issued .
(3 ) This Act shall become effective on January 1 , 1950 .
Not Approved .
Received by Secretary of State March 30 , 1948 .

CHAPTER 45
(H. B. 195 )
AN ACT relating to taxation of gasoline and prescribing the purposes
for which such tax may be expended.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
1. Section 138.220 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is
amended to read as follows :
An excise tax of seven ( 7c ) cents per gallon shall be
paid on all gasoline received in this state . Two - sevenths
(2/7 ) of all amounts received by reason of said tax shall
be set aside by the Department of Highways for the con-
struction, reconstruction and maintenance of rural and sec-
ondary roads and for no other purpose . No other excise or
license tax shall be levied or assessed on gasoline by the
state or any political subdivision of the state . The tax here-
in imposed shall be paid by the dealer receiving the gaso-
line to the State Treasurer in the manner and within the
time specified in KRS 138.230 to 138.340 . Nothing herein
contained shall authorize or require the collection of the
tax upon any gasoline after it has been once taxed under the
provisions of this section .
2. Whereas , the first fiscal year for which appropria-
tions are made and set aside to the Department of Highways
by this Act will commence on April 1 , 1948 and it is essential
that the increased tax on gasoline become effective at the
beginning of such fiscal year .
NOW , THEREFORE , an emergency is declared to exist
and this act shall take effect on April 1 , 1948 .
Approved Feb. 6 , 1948 .

classification.summary: This act amends Section 138.220 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes to increase the excise tax on gasoline to seven cents per gallon. Two-sevenths of the revenue generated by this tax is to be used exclusively for the construction, reconstruction, and maintenance of rural and secondary roads. The act also specifies that no other excise or license tax on gasoline shall be levied by the state or its political subdivisions. It declares an emergency and sets the effective date for April 1, 1948.

the highway system , and shall endeavor to enter into agree-

ary and rural roads for construction , reconstruction or main-
tenance as a part of the highway system of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky .

CHAPTER 47
(H. B. 197 )
AN ACT relating to local option with respect to the sale , barter or loan
of alcoholic beverages .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
1. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is
created , to be numbered KRS 242.125 , and to read as follows :
( 1 ) In any county in which the provisions of KRS
242.220 to 242.430 are applicable by reason of an election for
the entire county having heretofore resulted in favor of pro-
hibition , an election may be held in any city of the first four
classes in such county to take the sense of the people of such
city as to the discontinuance of prohibition in such city . For
this purpose , the election in such city shall not be deemed to
be an election in the " same territory " as that in which the
county -wide election was held , within the meaning of sub-
section (5 ) of KRS 242.030 . If , at such election for such a
city, the majority of the votes cast are against prohibition ,
the vote shall make KRS 242.220 to 242.430 inapplicable to
the entire city, but this shall not prevent an election from
thereafter being held in any precinct of such city, subject to
entries.citation: Acts of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky, 1948, p. 110

classification.title: AN ACT relating to local option with respect to the sale, barter or loan of alcoholic beverages.
classification.summary: This act creates a new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes (KRS 242.125) that allows for local option elections regarding the sale of alcoholic beverages. Specifically, in counties where prohibition is already in effect, cities of the first four classes within those counties can hold an election to decide whether to discontinue prohibition within the city limits. This city election is not considered an election in the 'same territory' as the county-wide prohibition election.
classification.reasoning: The statute deals with local option elections concerning the sale of alcoholic beverages. It allows for cities within dry counties to vote on whether to end prohibition within their city limits. There is no mention of race, nor are there any provisions that implicitly or explicitly discriminate based on race. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law.

It also specifies ballot formatting for prohibition propositions.

CHAPTER 48
(H. B. 199 )
AN ACT creating an Agricultural and Industrial Development Board ;
prescribing the powers and duties of said Board and making an
appropriation for the operation thereof .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. (a ) There is hereby created , as an independent
agency of the state government , a Board to be known as the
Agricultural and Industrial Development Board of Ken-
tucky (hereinafter referred to in this Act as the Board ) . The
Board shall be composed of not more than nine ( 9 ) members ,
who shall be appointed by the Governor and who shall hold
office at the pleasure of the Governor , and one of whom
shall from time to time be designated as chairman by the
Governor .
(b) The members of the Board shall be chosen for their
special qualifications as representatives of industry , agri-
culture , labor and other occupational and civic groups in-
terested in the agricultural and industrial development of
the Commonwealth . The members of the Board shall serve
without compensation but shall be reimbursed for their ex-
penses incurred while attending meetings of the Board or
otherwise discharging their official duties .
(c ) The Board shall meet at the call of the chairman ,
at such time and place as shall be designated in the call , and
entries.citation: Acts of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky, 1948, p. 113

classification.title: AN ACT creating an Agricultural and Industrial Development Board
classification.summary: This act creates the Agricultural and Industrial Development Board of Kentucky as an independent state agency. The Board will consist of up to nine members appointed by the Governor, chosen for their expertise in industry, agriculture, labor, and other relevant groups. Members will serve without compensation but will be reimbursed for expenses. The Board's purpose is to promote agricultural and industrial development in the Commonwealth.

The first is a note about a repealed statute and election ballot formatting, which is not relevant to Jim Crow laws. The second, Chapter 48, establishes an Agricultural and Industrial Development Board. This statute focuses on economic development and does not contain any language, explicit or implicit, that suggests racial segregation or discrimination. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law.

§ 2. It shall be the duty of the Board , through its
Director , to prepare and execute such a program of research ,
publicity and advertising as , in its discretion , will attract
favorable attention to the industrial , commercial , recrea-
tional and agricultural potentialities and facilities of the
Commonwealth . The Board , through its Director , shall assist
and coordinate the activities of the various departments and
agencies of the state government and of the various munici-
palities , political subdivisions , civic groups , and other
agencies interested and active in the promotion and develop-
ment of industry , agriculture , mining , and any of the other
potentialities of the Commonwealth . The Board shall aid
and encourage private organizations and groups in such
promotion and development .
§ 3. (a) The Board , through its Director , shall pre-
pare , assemble and publish as soon as practicable , an inven-
tory of the human and natural resources of the Common-
wealth ; and shall assemble and keep in the offices of the
Board such information relating to natural resources , labor
supply , water, fuel , power, taxation and assessments , trans-
portation and communication , and such other information as
may be useful in effectuating the provisions of this Act . Such
information shall , subject to the rules and regulations of the
Board (the power to promulgate which is hereby given ) , be
made available to any enterprise which in the discretion of
the Board is legitimate and which contemplates location or
operation within the Commonwealth . Such information shall,
in the discretion of the Board , be made available to any gov-
ernmental or private agency interested in securing the loca-
tion or operation of any such legitimate enterprise within the
Commonwealth .

classification.title: An Act relating to the promotion of the Commonwealth of Kentucky
classification.summary: This act establishes a Board to promote the industrial, commercial, recreational, and agricultural development of Kentucky. The Board, through a Director, is tasked with preparing and executing programs of research, publicity, and advertising. It will also coordinate efforts with state departments, municipalities, civic groups, and private organizations. The Board will compile an inventory of the Commonwealth's resources and make relevant information available to legitimate enterprises considering locating or operating in Kentucky, as well as to agencies interested in attracting such enterprises.

Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law. The document type is inferred as 'session_laws' because it is presented as a chapter from a legislative session (Gen Assemb 1948.pdf).

classification.summary: This act establishes a board responsible for gathering information on enterprises interested in locating in the Commonwealth, preparing and publishing planning studies and surveys related to various aspects of the Commonwealth's development (including land use, population, facilities, transportation, and urban development), and consulting with various agencies to coordinate development plans and make recommendations. The board's duties include studying land utilization for various purposes and promoting the use of resources.

(1 ) The jury commissioners shall take the last re-
turned tax commissioner's book for the county ; except in
counties containing a city of the second class and a court of
continuous session , in which counties the jury commissioners
shall take the last returned registered election voters book
for the county or last Returned Tax Commissioner Book ; and
from it shall carefully select from the intelligent , sober , dis-
creet and impartial citizens , resident housekeepers in differ-
ent portions of the county , over twenty -one years of age , the
following number of names of such persons :
(a) In counties having a population of ten thousand or
less , not less than one hundred and sixty -two nor more than
two hundred .
(b) In counties having a population of more than ten
thousand and not more than twenty thousand , not less than
one hundred and sixty-two nor more than three hundred .
(c) In counties having a population of more than twenty
thousand and not more than fifty thousand , not less than five
hundred nor more than six hundred .
(d ) In counties having a population of more than fifty
thousand and not more than one hundred thousand, one
thousand .
( e ) In counties having a population of more than one
hundred thousand , and its circuit court divided into branches ,
two thousand for each division of the court requiring the
services of a jury .
( 2 ) They shall place the name , and in counties con-
taining a city of the first , second or third class , the address ,
of each person so selected , on a small slip of paper, each slip
being as near the same size and appearance as practicable .
In counties having courts of continuous session in which the
commissioners have employed a typist , the typist shall type
the names on the slips ; in other counties the commissioners
shall write the name on the slips in plain handwriting . Each
slip shall be by them enclosed in a small case and deposited ,
unsealed , in a revolving drum , but before depositing any of
the slips in the drum they shall carefully examine its con-
tents and remove and destroy any slips found therein .

The act also details the number of names to be selected based on county population and the procedure for preparing and depositing these names for jury duty.

CHAPTER 50
(H. B. 202 )
AN ACT relating to the taking of mussels and the buying of mussel
shells including provisions for licenses and license fees .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
1. A new section of the statutes is created , to be
numbered KRS 150.600 and to read as follows :
(1 ) No person may engage in the buying of mussel
shells or mussels in the shell in this state unless he has in
his possession a license authorizing him so to do . The annual
fee for such license shall be $ 100 for a resident of this state ,
and $300 for a nonresident of this state . Such licenses shall
be issued by the Division of Game and Fish either directly
or through such county clerks as it may designate for the
purpose .
(2 ) Any person who violates this section shall be fined
not less than $25 nor more than $200 or imprisoned for not
more than six months , or both .
§ 2. Subsection ( 2 ) of Section 150.220 of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes is amended to read as follows :

CHAPTER 52
(H. B. 207)
AN ACT relating to traps and the trapping of animals .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
SECTION 1. Section 150.400 of the Kentucky Re-
vised Statutes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) No person shall set , use or maintain , for the pur-
pose of taking wild animals , any steel trap unless the size
and type of the trap has been approved , in writing , by the
Director of the Division of Game and Fish . The Director
shall not approve any steel trap :
(a) That has a jaw- spread of more than 54 inches , or
the jaws of which have sharp or jagged edges ; or
(b) That has more than one spring for the purpose of
closing the jaws or that has a spring which would normally
be expected , under ordinary circumstances , to exert a pres-
sure sufficient instantly to cut through or break the leg of
any wild animal protected by this chapter ; or
(c) The design of which is such that animals may
escape easily therefrom with permanent or fatal injuries ; or
(d ) That does not have a swivel as an integral part of
the trap or the retaining chain .
( 2) The Director shall approve any commercially manu-
factured trap which is designed so as to take animals alive
unhurt or to kill instantly .
(3 ) Subject to the provisions of KRS 150.410 , it shall
be lawful to use snares , deadfalls, wire -cage or box traps ,
but no person shall set , use or maintain a snare large enough
to take deer , elk , bear or other large animals .
(4) Manufacturers of and dealers in or persons pro-
posing to use steel traps may submit sample traps and speci-

classification.title: AN ACT relating to traps and the trapping of animals.
classification.summary: This act amends Section 150.400 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes concerning the use of steel traps and other trapping devices. It specifies conditions under which steel traps may be approved by the Director of the Division of Game and Fish, focusing on jaw spread, spring mechanisms, and the potential for animal injury. It also permits the use of snares, deadfalls, wire-cage, or box traps under certain conditions, prohibiting snares large enough for deer, elk, bear, or other large animals.
classification.reasoning: The statute pertains to regulations for trapping animals and does not contain any language or provisions that suggest racial discrimination or segregation. It focuses solely on the technical specifications and legal use of trapping devices. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law.

CHAPTER 52 107
fications thereof to the Director for approval . From time to
time the Director shall file with the Secretary of State a list
of traps approved by him , stating the name of the manu-
facturer , the size , number and other information sufficient
to identify the trap , and shall furnish copies of such lists
to each County Court Clerk and to any other person request-
ing copies thereof . At least ninety days prior to the beginning
of each open season for trapping fur -bearing animals the Di-
rector annually shall cause to be published once in at least
two daily newspapers of general circulation in this state , a
list of all traps which have been approved by him.
(5 ) If the Director finds that a trap , a sample of the
specifications of which are submitted to him , does not meet
the requirements of this section and any valid regulation
adopted hereunder , he shall notify , in writing , the person
submitting same of his action and state his reasons thereof .
Within thirty days from the date of the Director's ruling ,
the person seeking the Director's approval may appeal there-
from to the Game and Fish Commission , which shall hold a
public hearing thereon . If the Commission sustains the
Director , the applicant , within thirty days from the date of
the order of the Commission , may appeal to the Franklin
Circuit Court by filing a copy of the order of the Commission ,
together with a copy of the record made before the Com-
mission . The order of the Commission shall be affirmed if
there is any competent evidence supporting it .
SECTION 2. Section 150.410 of the Kentucky Re-
vised Statutes is amended to read as follows :
(1 ) No person shall set a trap on the land of another
without written permission from the owner of the land , and
he shall exhibit such written consent to anyone lawfully de-
manding to examine it.
(2 ) No person shall set , use or maintain a trap for the
purpose of taking wild animals unless there is attached
thereto a metal tag giving the name and address of the per-
son setting , using or maintaining the trap . The Commission.
is authorized to furnish such tags , at cost, to applicants
therefor .

(3 ) Each person who sets , uses or maintains a trap or

sion , is authorized to make regulations to carry out and en-
force the provisions and purposes of this Act .
SECTION 5. All laws or parts of laws in conflict with
the provisions of this Act are hereby repealed .

$ 1. Two or more adjacent counties may , by a con-
tract approved by order of the fiscal court of each of the
counties , provide for the establishment and operation of a
joint poorhouse or poor farm, or combined poorhouse and
poor farm . The contract may provide for the use of the exist-
ing lands and buildings owned and operated by one of the
participating counties for poorhouse or poor farm purposes ,
with the title to remain in such county , or it may provide that
one of the participating counties may convey to the other
counties , a proportionate interest in the existing lands and
buildings of such county . The contract may further provide
that one or more or all of the participating counties shall
liquidate its lands and buildings , used for poorhouse or poor
farm purposes , and use the proceeds for the purpose of con-
tributing to the establishment and operation of the joint
project . In the event the contract shall provide for the ac-
quisition of new lands or buildings , the ownership of the new
lands and buildings may be divided among the participating
counties in proportion to the contributions made by each
county to the joint project , or the ownership may be vested
in the board of trustees for the joint project , as trustees for
the participating counties .
$§ 2. Any contract entered into pursuant to the provi-
sions of Section 1 of this Act shall provide for apportioning
the expenses of establishing and operating the joint project ,
between the participating counties , in proportion to the tax-
able property in each county or in such other proportion as
may be agreed upon , taking into consideration the original
contribution by each county to the joint project .
§ 3. Any contract entered into pursuant to the provi-
sions of Section 1 of this Act shall provide for the appoint-
ment of a board of trustees for the joint project , to be com-
posed of one member from each of the participating counties ,
appointed by the county judge , and , in the event the num-
ber of participating counties is an even number , one addi-
tional member to be appointed by the other members . The
members may be appointed for such terms , not exceeding
four years , as may be provided in the contract , and pro-
vision may be made for staggered terms . Vacancies shall be

The primary purpose is to facilitate cooperative efforts in providing for the poor.

CHAPTER 54
(H. B. 215 )
AN ACT providing for payments from the proceeds of the sale of elec-
trical energy , of water and of gas service by publicly owned light ,
water or gas plants to the boards of education of public school dis-
tricts in which the property or properties of such publicly owned
light , or water or gas plants may be located, and providing a method
of determining the amount thereof .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Each board of education of a public school
district in which is located the property or properties of a
publicly owned light , water, or gas plant may each year be
paid by the governing board of such plant from the proceeds
of the sale of electrical energy , water or gas an amount which
shall not exceed that determined by multiplying the book
value of the property or properties of such publicly owned
light , water or gas plant as of the beginning of each year by
the current tax rate levied for school purposes for the school
district in which such property or properties may be located .
"Book value , " as used in this section , means the cost of tangi-

CHAPTER 56 113
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Sec . 1. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created , to be numbered KRS 163.035 and to read as fol-
lows :
( 1 ) The Division of Vocational Rehabilitation in the
Department of Education , with the approval of the State
Board of Education , is authorized to establish a business en-
terprises program for the blind . In connection therewith, the
division shall be authorized to own , lease , manage , supervise
and operate vending stands and other business enterprises
for the use or benefit of blind persons , in federal , state ,
private and other buildings , provided however that the total
expenditure for the establishing of any new enterprise shall
not be more than twice the amount contributed by the U. S.
Government . The share of the supervisory and other expenses
of this program to be charged to each enterprise shall not ex-
ceed 3 percent ( 3 % ) of the gross receipts of such enterprise
above the amount of $ 125.00 of gross sales per week , the first
$ 125.00 per week being exempt from any such fee .
( 2 ) In connection with this program , no fee or service
charge or any other type of payment shall be made to any
company , firm , or society , for supervisory , engineering or
accounting services .
( 3 ) All sums received by the Division of Vocational
Rehabilitation in the form of receipts from the operation of
the program , or in the form of federal grants , shall be used
for the continuation of the program . Nothing in this Act is
intended to authorize the Division to expend , or incur any
obligation for , any sum other than has been specifically appro-
priated by the General Assembly to such Division , and other
than is available through the revolving fund .

AN ACT to repeal , amend, and re-enact Section 183.390 , Kentucky
Revised Statutes, by giving authority to the city or county , or city
and county , to establish , construct , maintain , manage , operate and
control an airport .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
That Section 183.390 , Kentucky Revised Statutes be , and
the same is hereby repealed , amended and re-enacted so as to
read as follows , viz .:
( 1 ) The city or county or city and county may by
ordinance or resolution , provide for the appointment of an
airport board composed of six members who shall have
authority to establish , construct , maintain , manage , operate
and control the airport . The legislative body of the city or
county or the city and county shall by adoption of an ordinance
or resolution , prescribe the rules and regulations by which
the board shall be governed so as to carry out the intent and
purpose of this act .
(2 ) The members of the airport board shall be citizens ,
taxpayers and legal voters of the county , and no member of
the board shall be interested directly or indirectly in any
contract for the furnishing of supplies or of service of any
kind to the airport .
(3 ) The first airport board appointed shall serve for
terms as follows : Two for one year , two for two years , and
two for three years . Upon the expiration of the first terms ,
successors shall be appointed for a term of three years . In
the case of an airport jointly owned by a city and county the

AN ACT relating to the tax on premiums received by insurance carriers
for workmen's compensation insurance ; amending KRS 342.445 and
342.450 .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Section 342.445 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
Every insurance carrier , including the parties to any
interindemnity contract or reciprocal plan or scheme , insuring
employers in this state against liability for personal injuries
to their employes , or death caused thereby under this chapter ,
shall pay a tax upon the premium received , whether in cash
or notes , in this state or on account of business done in this
state , for such insurance in this state , at the rate of two
percent of the amount of the premium. This tax shall be in
lieu of all other taxes on such premiums . It shall be assessed
and collected as provided in KRS 342.450 . Such insurance
carriers shall be credited with all canceled or returned pre-
miums , including dividends paid or credited to policyholders ,
and with premiums received for reinsurance assumed from
companies authorized and licensed to transact business in
this state .
§ 2. Section 342.450 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
Every such insurance carrier shall make an annual return
verified by the affidavit of its president and secretary , or other
chief officer or agents , to the Director of the Division of
Insurance , stating the amount of all such premiums and

CHAPTER 61
(H. B. 254 )
AN ACT providing for the payment of certain expenses to the Judges
and Commissioners of the Court of Appeals and the Circuit Judges
of the Commonwealth ; imposing a tax on civil suits to further the
purposes of the Act ; creating a Revolving Fund ; providing for the
advancement to the Revolving Fund from the General Fund the
sum of Seventy -Five Thousand Dollars ( $ 75,000.00 ) , and its repay-
ment to the General Fund ; and declaring an emergency to exist .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Each Circuit Court Judge of the State shall
be paid out of the State Treasury , each month , the sum of
$100.00 , which sum is hereby declared to be the equivalent of
the minimum sum that each Judge will expend each month , in
the performance of his official duties , for postage , telephone
service , supplies , stationery, stenographic assistance , law
clerk hire , and books and periodicals . The Commissioner of
Finance shall , on the first of each month , draw his warrant
on the Treasurer in favor of each Circuit Court Judge , for
the sum above stated , and the same shall be paid out of the
Revolving Fund created by Section 4 of this Act . The sums
so received shall be expended by each Circuit Court Judge in
his discretion , the manner in which the same shall be applied
in payment or reimbursement of such expenses being deter-
mined by the Judge ,

equivalent of the travel , food and lodging expenses which such
Judge would incur , in the performance of his official duties , if
compelled to travel to and from the place of his legal residence .
When a Judge or Commissioner has established a temporary
place of abode at the State Capital , or a place convenient
thereto , he shall certify that fact to the Commissioner of
Finance , and payment of the monthly allowance shall then
commence and shall thereafter continue so long as the Judge
or Commissioner remains in office and continues to maintain
an actual place of abode in the State Capital or a place con-
venient thereto . Said payment shall be made out of the
Revolving Fund created by Section 4 of this Act .

CHAPTER 63
(H. B. 257)
AN ACT relating to motor carriers , with particular reference to tax on
passenger vehicles operated in the capacity of industrial busses .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 281.520 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
(1 ) Except as provided in subsection ( 2 ) of this section ,
every common carrier and every contract carrier shall pay
to the Director of the Division of Motor Transportation , for
each motor vehicle operated by such carrier for the transpor-
tation of persons under a certificate or permit , a mileage tax
equal to one-thirty -sixth of a cent for each regular seat in the
vehicle multiplied by the total number of miles traveled , and ,
in addition , a tag tax equal to eight dollars for each regular
seat for the first sixteen seats , fifteen dollars for each regular
seat for the next eight seats , and twenty -five dollars for each
regular seat for all remaining seats .
( 2 ) ( a ) The mileage tax and tag tax imposed by sub-
section ( 1 ) of this section do not apply to taxicabs , city busses
and U-drive -its , nor to vehicles operating under a certificate
in interstate commerce where such vehicle does not enter the
State of Kentucky a distance of more than ten miles from the
point of entry into this state , but a tax of one dollar and fifty
cents for each regular seat in such vehicle shall be paid .

classification.title: AN ACT relating to motor carriers, with particular reference to tax on passenger vehicles operated in the capacity of industrial busses.
classification.summary: This act amends Section 281.520 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes concerning motor carrier taxes. It establishes a mileage tax and a tag tax based on the number of regular seats in passenger vehicles operated by common and contract carriers. Certain vehicles like taxicabs, city buses, U-drive-its, and vehicles operating in interstate commerce under specific conditions are exempted or have different tax rates. The act specifies tax rates for different numbers of seats.
classification.reasoning: The statute concerns taxation of motor carriers and passenger vehicles. It details mileage and tag taxes based on vehicle capacity and usage. There is no mention of race, nor any provisions that implicitly or explicitly discriminate based on race. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law.

CHAPTER 63 123
(b) The tag tax imposed by subsection ( 1 ) of this sec-
tion does not apply to industrial busses , but a tax of two
dollars and fifty cents for each regular seat in such busses
shall be paid . For the purpose of this paragraph , " industrial
bus " means a motor vehicle designed for the transportation
of persons for hire which is used exclusively for the trans-
portation of the employes of an industry to and from their
place of employment , and which is operated over a route not
exceeding thirty miles in length .
(3) The term " regular seat , " as used in this section
and in KRS 281.530 , means a seat ordinarily and customarily
used by one passenger , but does not include folding or col-
lapsible emergency aisle seats , provided that the number of
such folding or collapsible emergency aisle seats shall not be
greater in number than twenty-five percent of the regular
seats in the vehicle . Passenger vehicles of the regular passen-
ger automobile type shall be considered to have three regular
seats on the rear seat or any like seat , one regular seat with
the driver and two regular seats for any collapsible seat
between the rear seat and the driver's seat . Where any seats
in the vehicle are not distinguished by separate cushions and
backs , a seat shall be counted for each eighteen -inch space
on such seats or major portion thereof.
Section 2. A new section of the statutes is created , to
read as follows :
No vehicle represented to be , and licensed as , an industrial
bus as defined in Paragraph (b ) of Subsection ( 1 ) of KRS
281.520 , shall be used or permitted to be used in any trans-
portation of persons for hire other than the transportation
of employes of an industry to and from their place of employ-
ment over a route not exceeding thirty miles in length . In case
of violation of this section , the person in whose name the
vehicle is licensed shall be fined not less than fifteen dollars
nor more than two hundred dollars , or imprisoned in jail for
not more than thirty days , or both ; in addition , such person
shall forfeit all certificates and permits held by him under
KRS Chapter 281 and shall not be eligible to hold any certifi-
cate or permit under KRS Chapter 281 for a period of five
years thereafter .
entries.citation: Acts of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky, 1948, p. 138

classification.title: CHAPTER 63 123
classification.summary: This statute defines and regulates 'industrial buses' used for transporting industry employees to and from work. It exempts these buses from the general tag tax but imposes a per-seat tax. It also specifies penalties for using an industrial bus for any other transportation of persons for hire. The statute details how 'regular seats' are counted, including provisions for folding seats and seats without distinct cushions. It also outlines penalties for violations, including fines, imprisonment, and forfeiture of permits.
classification.reasoning: The statute concerns the regulation of industrial buses and their taxation. It defines what constitutes an 'industrial bus' and sets rules for its usage, specifically for transporting industry employees. The penalties for violating these rules are also detailed. There is no mention of race, nor are there any provisions that implicitly suggest racial segregation or discrimination. Therefore, this statute is not a Jim Crow law. CHAPTER 64
( H. B. 260 )
AN ACT to repeal , amend and re- enact Sections 342.005 , 342.020 , 342.025 ,
342.040 , 342.055 , 342.070 , 342.080 , 342.095 , 342.100 , 342.105 , 342.110 ,
342.120 , 342.230 , 342.270 , and 342.395 , Kentucky Revised Statutes ,
relating to Workmen's Compensation .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. That Section 342.005 , Kentucky Revised
Statutes be repealed , amended and re -enacted so that same ,
when re -enacted shall read as follows :
( 1 ) This chapter shall apply to all employers having
three or more employes regularly engaged in the same occupa-
tion or business , and to their employes , except that it shall not
apply to domestic employment , agriculture , steam railways ,
or such common carriers other than steam railways for which
a rule of liability is provided by the laws of the United States .
Provided , however , it shall apply to the operators of threshing
machines used in threshing or hulling grain or seeds . It shall
affect the liability of the employers subject thereto to their
employes for a personal injury sustained by the employe by
accident arising out of and in the course of his employment ,
or for death resulting from such accidental injury ; provided ,
however , that personal injury by accident as herein defined
shall not include diseases except where the disease is the
natural and direct result of a traumatic injury by accident ,
nor shall it include the results of a pre-existing disease ,
whether previously disabling or not, but shall include injury

CHAPTER 64 125
or death due to inhalation in mines of noxious gases or smoke ,
commonly known as " bad air," and also shall include injuries
or death due to the inhalation of any kind of gas .
( 2 ) Any employers and employes who are by the pro-
visions of this section excepted from the provisions of this
chapter , including employers having less than three employes ,
may subject themselves thereto by joint , voluntary application
to the board, in writing , for such period as may be stated in
the application , which shall be irrevocable during such period
and effective thereafter until a written revocation be filed with
the board or the employment be terminated , and any em-
ployers and their employes may , with respect to the disease
of silicosis caused by the inhalation of silica dust, in like
manner voluntarily subject themselves thereto as to such
disease .
Section 2. That Section 342.020 , Kentucky Revised
Statutes , be repealed , amended and re -enacted so that same ,
when re - enacted , shall read as follows :
( 1 ) In addition to all other compensation provided in
this chapter , the employer shall furnish for the cure and
relief from the effects of an injury , such medical , surgical and
hospital treatment , including nursing , medical and surgical
supplies and appliances , as may reasonably be required at the
time of the injury and thereafter during disability , but not
exceeding a total expense to the employer of more than five
hundred dollars . The Board may, by order made upon appli-
cation within a period of six months from date of the accident ,
extend the limit of expense to not exceeding a total of eight
hundred dollars . If the employer fails to furnish such treat-
ment reasonably , he shall be liable for the reasonable expense ,
within the limits of this section , incurred by or on behalf of
the employe in providing such treatments . In an emergency ,
the employe may call in any available physician or surgeon
to administer any first aid reasonably necessary at the expense
of the employer within the limits of this section . The Board ,
in its discretion , may apportion payments to be made under
this section between hospitals and physicians in cases where
their aggregate fees and charges would exceed the maximum
for which the employer is liable .

classification.title: An Act to amend Section 342.020, Kentucky Revised Statutes, relating to workmen's compensation.
classification.summary: This act amends Kentucky Revised Statutes Section 342.020 to clarify and expand employer liability for medical, surgical, and hospital treatment for injured employees. It specifies a maximum expense limit for such treatment, with provisions for extension by the Board, and outlines employer liability for reasonable expenses if they fail to provide adequate treatment. It also allows for emergency first aid at the employer's expense and grants the Board discretion to apportion payments between hospitals and physicians.

Section 6. That Section 342.070 , Kentucky Revised
Statutes , be repealed , amended and re -enacted so that same ,
when re -enacted shall read as follows :
If death should result within two years from an accident
for which compensation is payable under this chapter , the
employer or his insurer shall pay to the person entitled to
compensation , or , if none , then to the personal representative
of the deceased employee , reasonable burial expenses of a
person of the standard of living of the deceased , not to exceed
the sum of $300.00 , and shall also pay to or for the following
persons compensation as follows :
( 1 ) If there are no dependents there shall be paid , in
addition to the burial expenses and medical expenses , if any ,
the further sum of $ 200.00 , payment to be made to the personal
representative of the deceased employee .
( 2 ) If there are one or more wholly dependent persons
65 percent of the average weekly earnings of the deceased
employee , but not to exceed $ 20.00 nor less than $7.00 per week
shall be payable , all such payments to be made for the period
between the date of death and 400 weeks after the date of
accident to the employee , or until the intervening termination
of dependency , but in no case to exceed the maximum sum of
$8,000.00 .
( 3) (a ) If there are partly dependent persons , the
payments shall be such part of what would be payable for
total dependency as the partial dependency existing at the
time of the accident to the employee is proportionate to total
dependency and all such payments to be made for the period
between the date of death and 400 weeks after the date of the
accident to the deceased employee , or until the intervening
termination of dependency , but in no case to exceed in the
aggregate of compensation on account of such death the
maximum sum of $8,000.00 .

(2 ) In case of the following injuries the disability shall
be considered total and permanent :
( a ) The total permanent loss of sight in both eyes .
(b ) The loss of both feet at or above the ankle .
(c) The loss of both hands at or above the wrist .
(d) A similar loss of one hand and one foot .
(e ) An injury to the spine , resulting in permanent and
complete paralysis of both arms or both legs , or of one arm
and one leg .
(f) An injury to the skull resulting in incurable insanity
or imbecility .
(3) The enumeration in subsection ( 2 ) is not exclusive ,
but in all other cases the burden of proof shall be on the
claimant to prove that his injuries have resulted in permanent
total disability .
Section 9. That Section 342.100 , Kentucky Revised
Statutes , be repealed , amended and re-enacted so that same ,
when re -enacted , shall read as follows :
In case of an injury resulting in temporary partial dis-
ability , the employee shall receive during such disability ,
except the first seven days thereof , a weekly compensation
equal to 65 percent of the difference between his average
weekly earnings before the injury and the average weekly
earnings that he earns , or is able to earn , in some suitable
employment after the injury and during such disability , not
to exceed 450 weeks from the date of injury, nor exceeding
the sum of $18.00 per week nor the maximum sum of $8,100.00 .
If partial disability follows a period of total disability , the
period of total disability shall be deducted from the maximum
period allowed for partial disability .

CHAPTER 64 131
Section 10. That Section 342.105 , Kentucky Revised
Statutes, be repealed , amended and re-enacted so that same ,
when re-enacted , shall read as follows :
For injuries enumerated in the following schedule the
employe shall receive , in addition to temporary total disability
compensation for the period of actual total disability , not
exceeding 20 weeks , a weekly compensation equal to 65 percent
of his average weekly earnings, but not less than $7.00 per
week nor exceeding $18.00 per week for the periods stated
thereon :
( 1 ) For the loss of a thumb, 65 percent of the average
weekly wages during 60 weeks .
(2 ) For the loss of a first finger, commonly called the
index finger , 65 percent of the average weekly wages during
45 weeks .
(3 ) For the loss of a second finger, 65 percent of the
average weekly wages during 30 weeks .
(4) For the loss of a third finger, 65 percent of the
average weekly wages during 20 weeks .
(5 ) For the loss of a fourth finger , commonly known as
the little finger , 65 percent of the average weekly wages during
15 weeks .
(6) The loss of the second , or distal phalange , of the
thumb shall be considered to be equal to the loss of one-half
of the thumb ; the loss of more than one-half of the thumb
shall be considered equal to the loss of the whole thumb .
(7) The loss of the third , or distal phalange of any finger
shall be considered to be equal to the loss of one-third of the
finger.
( 8) The loss of the middle , or second phalange of any
finger shall be considered equal to the loss of two -thirds of
the finger .
( 9 ) The loss of more than the middle and distal pha-
langes of any finger shall be considered equal to the loss of
the whole finger, but in no case shall the amount received for
more than one finger exceed the amount provided in this
schedule for the loss of a hand .

Section 13. That Section 342.230 , Kentucky Revised

(1 ) Upon recommendation of the board and within the
limits of appropriations therefor , the Commissioner of Indus-

medical services and advice , necessary to carry on the board's
work .
(2 ) The Commissioner of Industrial Relations shall also
appoint an Executive Secretary of the Board who shall have
immediate supervision of the employees of the Board and
perform such duties as are assigned to him .. The Executive
Secretary shall be an attorney who has been admitted to
practice law in Kentucky and shall have practiced law for at
least three years . He shall keep and be the Custodian of the
records of the board , and shall devote his full time to the
duties of his office . He shall receive a salary of not exceeding
$4,500.00 per year as fixed by the Commissioner of Industrial
Relations .
(3 ) The board may at any time recommend the removal
of any appointee upon filing with the Commissioner of Indus-
trial Relations a full written statement of its reasons for such
removal .

utes is amended and reenacted and as so amended and re-

During December of each year or as soon as the rates for
the coming year are fixed , the tax bills shall be made out ac-
cording to the provisions of the ordinances levying taxes and
the following January shall be listed with the tax receiver for
collection . Taxes on personal property may be included with

erty therein named or described .
