Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 260.020 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is
amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) The Division of Markets in the Department of
Agriculture , Labor and Statistics shall be under the super-
visor of the commissioner , and shall consist of personnel
determined and appointed by the commissioner .
(2 ) The Division of Markets shall be headed by a
director appointed by the commissioner , subject to the ap-
proval of the Governor . The Director of the Division of Mar-
kets shall receive a salary of not more than $ 5,000 annually.
Approved March 13 , 1948 .
CHAPTER 139
(S. B. 51 )
AN ACT making provisions for an Agricultural Production and Market-
ing Commission ; prescribing its membership , powers and duties ;
providing for the furnishing of assistance , cooperation and facilities
by other state agencies ; and providing for a report .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
1. There is created an Agricultural Production and
Marketing Commission , to consist of fifteen members . The
Commissioner of Agriculture , Labor and Statistics shall be
ex-officio a member and chairman of the Commission . The
other fourteen members shall be appointed by the Governor ,
forthwith upon the taking effect of this Act . The Governor
shall designate one of the members appointed by him as vice-
chairman of the Commission . The Commission shall continue
in existence until December 31 , 1949 , whereupon its existence
shall terminate .

338 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
1. The legislative bodies of cities of the third and fourth
classes may , in their discretion , pay unto any member of the
police or fire departments , in case he is disabled from an
injury received while acting in his official capacity and in the
line of duty to such an extent as he is unable to execute the
duties of his official capacity or pursue a gainful occupation
for which he is fitted by training or experience , monthly
compensation benefits equal to said member's monthly earn-
ings at the time of his injury , so long as his disability con-
tinues , but in no event shall the total amount paid as com-
pensation benefits to any member exceed the sum of five
thousand dollars .
2. The legislative bodies of cities of the third and fourth
classes may , in their discretion , in addition to the compensa-
tion benefits provided for in Section 1 , pay the member's
medical and hospital expenses , but in no event shall the pay-
ments therefor exceed the sum of one thousand dollars .
3. This Act shall be construed to include any member
that is disabled to the extent provided for in Section 1 at the
time of its enactment due to an injury received under the
circumstances provided for in Section 1 within a period of
one year next prior to its passage , and all compensation ,
medical and hospital benefits and payments shall begin as of
the effective date of this Act .
4. The testimony of competent medical authorities shall
be the sole test as to whether any member is disabled to the
extent provided for in Section 1 .
5. This Act shall not be applicable in any city of the
third or fourth class which has in operation , pursuant to
statute , a pension system for policemen and firemen , under
which disability benefits are payable .
6. Whereas , there is an urgent need to provide adequate
disability benefits in order to secure competent personnel in
city police and fire departments , an emergency is hereby
declared to exist and this Act shall become effective upon its
passage and approval by the Governor of Kentucky .
Not approved .
Received by Secretary of State March 30 , 1948 .

CHAPTER 141
(S. B. 54)
AN ACT amending and re - enacting Sections 116.180 and 116.190 of
the Kentucky Revised Statutes, relating to the number of voters
in election precincts .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
1. Section 116.180 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is
amended and re -enacted to read as follows :
Each county shall be divided into election precincts . The
county court shall make the division , and shall establish the
name or number and the boundaries of the precincts . Each
precinct shall contain , as nearly as practicable , an equal
number of voters , based on the number of votes cast at the
last election for presidential electors . In case more than three
hundred and fifty votes were cast in a particular precinct at
the last presidential election , then the county court may , in
its discretion , if it is by the court deemed advisable for the
best interests of the public generally, redistrict the precincts
as equally as possible , but no precinct shall contain more than
five hundred and fifty votes .
This section is not intended to affect Section 125.180 of
the Kentucky Revised Statutes , relating to precincts wherein
voting machines are used or may be used .
2. Section 116.190 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is
amended and re -enacted to read as follows :
Whenever the public convenience or the public good
requires it, the county court of any county may change the
boundaries of any election precinct in the county , or divide
any precinct into two or more precincts , or consolidate two
or more precincts into one, but no change , division or con-
solidation shall be made after the June term of the court next
preceding an election . No change , division or consolidation
shall be valid unless due notice is given, at least one month
before any election , by one publication in the newspaper pub-
lished in the county that has the largest circulation therein ,
or by posters put up in four of the most public places in the
precinct .

The firemen's pension fund shall consist of :

for the use and purpose for which the fund is created . Any
gift , devise or bequest to the board of trustees , for the fund ,
may be absolute or upon the condition that only the rents ,
income or profits shall go to the fund .
(2) All fines from disciplinary action against any mem-
ber of the fire department .
( 3 ) All fines from violations of ordinances relative to

(4) All moneys , rewards or emoluments paid or given
to the fire department or any of the fire companies .
(5 ) All revenues received from exhibitions , field days
and other similar events promoted by members of the fire
department .

ment of two percent of his salary, to be deducted from the

CHAPTER 145
(S. B. 63 )
AN ACT relating to the publication of ordinances in cities of the second
class operating under the city manager form of government .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Ordinances adopted by the board of commissioners of
any city of the second class organized and governed under
the city manager form of government shall be published in
full in the newspaper with the largest bona fide circulation
published in the city when they relate to the raising of rev-
enue , the expenditure of public funds , or are police measures
that impose penalties for violations , except that in the case
of any ordinance adopting or enacting a standard United
States Government Code or a Commonwealth of Kentucky
departmental code, the ordinance as published need not set
such code forth at length , but may merely identify it by a
suitable reference . Other ordinances need not be published in
full , but the city clerk shall , within ten days after the adoption
of an ordinance , cause to be published in the newspaper with
the largest bona fide circulation an abstract thereof and shall
incorporate therein sufficient information to constitute a
description of the subject matter to which such ordinance
relates .

CHAPTER 146
(S. B. 64)
AN ACT providing for the appointment of an acting city manager in
cities of the second class organized under the city manager form
of government .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
The Board of Commissioners of any city of the second
class organized under the city manager form of government
may by ordinance appoint a suitable person who shall , at
any time when the city manager is absent from the city or
becomes disabled , act as city manager , and the ordinance
shall also fix the amount of his compensation or provide that
he shall serve without compensation . The acting city manager
while serving shall perform the same duties and shall be
vested with all of the powers of the city manager . In the
event of the death or resignation of the city manager , the
acting city manager shall serve until the position is filled .
An acting city manager shall not acquire any right or priv-
ilege under rules of civil service governing employees of
the city .
The acting city manager shall serve at the pleasure of
the board of commissioners , and may be removed at any time
by a majority of the board members .
Approved Feb. 27, 1948 .

CHAPTER 147 345
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Sub-section ( 2 ) of Section 89.590 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
( 2 ) Before a city of the second class shall enter into
any contract involving the expenditure of more than one
thousand dollars , with the exception of a contract for pro-
fessional services which shall not be let by competitive bid-
ding, the board of commissioners shall by resolution specify
the nature of the work to be contracted for , or the quality
and quantity of the supplies or materials to be purchased ,
and authorize the city manager to advertise for and receive
bids under seal for the execution of such work or the pur-
chase of such supplies or material . The city manager shall
advertise for bids by notice posted up at the city hall door
and by inserting notice of the request for bids in at least one
issue of a daily newspaper published in the city , and the
notices shall be published at least seven days prior to the
opening of bids . No bid shall be opened within ten days from
the date on which the resolution is adopted , and no bid shall
be received after the hour fixed for the opening of bids . All
bids received by the City Manager up to the time fixed by
the resolution for the opening of bids shall be filed with the
board of commissioners who shall let the contract to the
lowest responsible bidder . The City Manager may, for and
on behalf of the city , enter into any contract involving the
expenditure of one thousand dollars or less , but in the event
such contract involves the expenditure of more than five
hundred dollars he shall advertise for bids in the manner
provided in this subsection and let the contract to the lowest
responsible bidder, except that such contracts for profes-
sional services shall not be let by competitive bidding . The
board of commissioners or the City Manager may reject all
bids , in which event they may advertise for new bids in the
manner provided in this subsection .
Approved March 26 , 1948 .

CHAPTER 148
(S. B. 69 )
AN ACT amending and re-enacting Section 381.580 of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes relating to condemnation of private passways .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
1. That Section 381.580 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes relating to the condemnation of private passways , be
and the same is hereby amended and re -enacted by striking
therefrom the words, " which shall not exceed twenty feet " ,
and inserting in lieu thereof the following words , " which
shall not exceed sixty feet " , and that said section 381.580
when amended and re -enacted shall read as follows :
" Whenever it appears to a county court that it is neces-
sary for a person to have a private passway over the land of
one or more persons to enable him to attend courts , elections ,
a meeting house , a mill , warehouse , ferry , a railroad depot ,
most convenient to his residence , to have a private tramroad
or haul road over the land of one or more persons to enable
him to reach a warehouse , steamboat landing , ferry , railroad
switch or navigable stream , for the purpose of operating and
marketing the products from a lead mine , iron works , salt
works , coal mine , fire clay and other minerals , oil well , stone
quarry , sand bank or merchantable forest timber , the court
shall appoint commissioners , as in case of a road . The com-
missioners after being sworn to discharge their duties faith-
fully and impartially shall go upon the land through which
the passway , tramroad or haul road is proposed , whether
arable or not , and shall report in writing to the court whether
or not a private passway , tramroad or haul road is necessary
for any of the purposes aforesaid . If the commissioners are
favorable to the passway , tramroad or haul road , they shall ,
in their report , designate the exact route for the same by
metes and bounds , courses and distances , and the width there-
of, which shall not exceed sixty feet, and they shall determine
and assess what will be a just compensation to each owner
and tenant for the land proposed to be taken , in the same
manner as upon application to open and establish a new
road."

point all employes of the sinking fund , prescribe their duties

to the cost of administration of the fund , and to no other use
or purpose until the whole of the debts of the city are fully

indebtedness of the city.

(S. B. 78)
AN ACT relating to Banks and Trust Companies and Combined Banks
and Trust Companies , providing for Permissive Holidays , amend-
ing K. R. S. Section 356.085 and 356.193 to conform thereto and

Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. That a new section of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is hereby created providing for permissive holidays
for banks and trust companies and combined banks and trust
companies , said section to be numbered Section 287.195 and to
read as follows :
" 287.195 . Permissive Holidays . Any bank or trust com-
pany or combined bank and trust company may apply to the
Commissioner of Banking for permission to remain closed
on any day of the week fixed by the adoption of a resolution
of the Board of Directors of such institution .
If the Commissioner of Banking deems that the public
interest will be adequately served thereby , he shall grant to
such applying institution permission to remain closed on such
fixed day provided fifteen ( 15 ) days notice of such closing
be given the public by posting a statement to such effect in
some conspicuous place in said institution . Any day upon
which such bank or trust company or combined bank and trust
company shall, pursuant to such permission and notice , re-
main closed , shall in respect of such institution be deemed a
holiday for all purposes , and no bank or trust company or

including such holidays as are provided for in Section 287.195 ,
the instrument is payable on the next succeeding business
day. "
" 356.193 . Computation of Time when Day for Doing

ing any act herein required or permitted to be done falls on
Sunday or a holiday , including such holidays as are provided
for in Section 287.195 , the act may be done on the next suc-
ceeding secular or business day .

are hereby repealed to the extent of such conflict .

It clarifies that when a payment or condition is due on a holiday, it can be performed on the next business day. It also specifies that negotiable instruments maturing on a Sunday or holiday are payable on the next succeeding business day. The act also states that banks cannot close on any day during a week in which a public legal holiday occurs, as enumerated in KRS 2.110.
classification.reasoning: The statute deals with banking regulations and the handling of financial transactions on holidays. It does not contain any provisions that discriminate based on race, nor does it imply any racial segregation or differential treatment. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law. The category is null as it is not a Jim Crow law.

CHAPTER 151
(S. B. 80 )
AN ACT to repeal , amend and re -enact Section 342.121 ; to repeal , amend
and re -enact Section 342.185 ; to repeal , amend and re - enact Section
342.315 ; to repeal , amend and re -enact Section 342.316 ; all being
sections of the Kentucky Revised Statutes relating to Workmen's
Compensation ; and providing for an emergency clause . This Act
shall be known as the Lyon , Hayes , Cline , Lowman and Rayburn Bill .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
1. That Section 342.121 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes be repealed , amended and re -enacted so that the same
shall read as follows :
(a ) If on a claim for compensation for disability result-
ing from a subsequent injury by accident any medical question
shall be in controversy , or a determination thereof necessary
to the proper apportionment of liability therefor , the Board
shall refer the case to a panel appointed by it of not less than
two nor more than three disinterested physicians in the terri-
tory where the controversy occurred , for the purpose of in-
vestigating and reporting to the Board on the medical ques-
tions involved . The panel shall make its report to the
Workmen's Compensation Board in writing with respect to
all medical questions at issue or necessary to the apportion-
ment of disability between the subsequent injury and the prior
existing disability at the time of employment . The report
shall show the date of prior disablement which , if in dispute ,
shall be deemed a medical question .
( b ) The physicians appointed as provided in sub - section
( a ) of this section shall be allowed necessary traveling ex-
penses and reasonable fees , to be fixed by the Board but not
to exceed a fee of twenty dollars to each physician for making
such examination and report , plus a reasonable allowance for
expenses incurred in making such examination . The Board
may also allow traveling expenses to the employee . Such fees
and expenses so approved by the Board shall be paid out of
the Subsequent Injury Fund in cases for subsequent injuries .
(c) The medical panel shall, as soon as practicable after
it has completed its consideration of the case, report in writ-

Whereas , there are now pending numerous claims for

CHAPTER 152 357
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky , as follows :
That Section 342.285 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
be repealed and re - enacted to read as follows :
( 1 ) An award or order of the board as provided in KRS
342.275 , if application for review is not filed as provided for
in KRS 342.280 , shall be conclusive and binding as to all ques-
tions of fact , but either party may , within twenty ( 20 ) days
after the rendition of such final award or order of the board,
by petition appeal to the circuit court that would have juris-
diction to try an action for damages for the injuries if this
chapter did not exist , for the review of such order or award ,
the board and the adverse party being made respondents .
(2 ) The petition shall state fully the grounds upon which
a review is sought , assign all errors relied on and be verified
by the petitioner , who shall furnish copies of the petition to
the respondents at the time of filing it. Summons shall issue
upon the petition directing the adverse party to file answer
within fifteen ( 15 ) days after service thereof and directing
the board to send its entire original record , properly bound ,
to the clerk of the circuit court, after certifying that such rec-
ord is its entire original record, which shall be filed by the
clerk of the circuit court and such record shall then become
and be considered by the circuit court on the review.
(3) No new or additional evidence may be introduced in
the circuit court except as to the fraud or misconduct of some
person engaged in the administration of this chapter and
affecting the order , ruling or award, but the court shall other-
wise hear the cause upon the record as certified by the board
and shall dispose of the cause in summary manner , its review
being limited to determining whether or not :
(a ) The board acted without or in excess of its powers ;
(b) The order , decision or award was procured by fraud ;
(c) The order , decision or award is not in conformity to
the provisions of this chapter ; and
( d ) If findings of fact are in issue , whether such findings
of fact support the order , decision or award .
entries.citation: Acts of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky, 1948, p. 372

classification.title: CHAPTER 152 357
classification.summary: This statute amends KRS 342.285, outlining the process for appealing awards or orders made by a board. It specifies that such awards are conclusive on questions of fact unless an appeal is filed within twenty days to the circuit court. The appeal process involves filing a petition, serving respondents, and the board sending its original record for the court to review. New evidence is generally not allowed, except in cases of fraud or misconduct in the administration of the chapter. The court's review is limited to determining if the board acted within its powers, if the order was procured by fraud, if it conforms to the chapter's provisions, and if findings of fact support the order.
classification.reasoning: The statute provided details the procedural aspects of appealing decisions made by a board, specifically within the context of the Kentucky Revised Statutes (KRS 342.285). It outlines the steps for filing an appeal, the scope of review by the circuit court, and the limitations on introducing new evidence. There is no mention of race, racial classifications, or any provisions that would suggest discriminatory intent or application based on race. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law. The document type is 'codes' because it refers to and amends a section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes.

2. That Section 342.290 of Kentucky Revised Statutes
be repealed and re -enacted so that the same shall read as
follows :
( 1 ) Where an amount sufficient under Kentucky Re-
vised Statute 21.060 to authorize an appeal to the Court of
Appeals is involved , the judgment of the circuit court shall
be subject to appeal to the Court of Appeals . The scope of
review by the Court of Appeals shall include all matters sub-
ject to review by the circuit court and also errors of law aris-
ing in the circuit court and upon appeal made reviewable by
the civil code of procedure where not in conflict with this
chapter .
( 2 ) The procedure as to appeal to the Court of Appeals
shall be the same as in civil actions , so far as it is applicable
to and not in conflict with this chapter , except as follows :
(a ) The appellant shall file with the clerk of the circuit
court, together with proof or acknowledgement of service , a
schedule which indicates the portions of the record to be in-
corporated into the transcript of the record to be used on
appeal to the Court of Appeals . Should the appellee or his
counsel desire additional portions of the record incorporated
into the record to be filed in the Court of Appeals , he may
file with the clerk of the circuit court his schedule also with-
in ten ( 10 ) days thereafter ( unless the time is extended by
order of the circuit court or the Court of Appeals ) , indicat-
ing the additional portions of the record desired by him.
Provided , however , that if the original record of the Work-
men's Compensation Board is desired by either party on
appeal to the Court of Appeals , it shall not be necessary for
the clerk of the circuit court to copy said original record into
the transcript , but the same shall be and become a part of the

CHAPTER 153 359
transcript and shall be forwarded to the Clerk of the Court
of Appeals as a part of the record upon appeal to said court .
The Clerk shall not be entitled to any transcript fee for such
original record.
(3 ) The clerk of the circuit court will transmit to the
Court of Appeals as the transcript of the record only those
portions of the record of the lower court as are designated
by the parties as above .
3. That section 342.295 be repealed and re -enacted so
that the same shall read as follows :
The circuit court or the Court of Appeals , may make
such rule or order as it may deem necessary for the safe
keeping , transporting and return of such original records
of the Workmen's Compensation Board to the said board
when the judgment in the case becomes final . When any ap-
peal has become final the Clerk of the Court of Appeals shall
return such original record to the Workmen's Compensation
Board , who shall safely keep the same . At all times there-
after such record shall remain a part of the record of the
case in the Court of Appeals , though kept in the possession
of the Workmen's Compensation Board .
Whereas , it appears that in numerous instances the rec-
ords in cases before the Workmen's Compensation Board are
unusually large , requiring considerable time to copy the en-
tire record for appeal to the circuit court, and likewise re-
quiring considerable time to copy the record for the Court
of Appeals , all of which in many instances causes unusual
delay in the final determination of such cases , an emergency
is hereby declared to exist and provisions of this act shall
become effective immediately upon its passage and approval
by the Governor .
Approved March 13 , 1948 .

CHAPTER 154
(S. B. 85 )
AN ACT amending Sub - section ( 3 ) of Section 90.320 of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes , relating to civil service in cities of the second
class .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Sub-section (3 ) of Section 90.320 of the Kentucky Re-
vised Statutes is amended to read as follows :
Every soldier , sailor , marine , members of the air forces ,
Army Nurses Corps and members of other branches of the
military services and Red Cross nurses who served during
the period of hostilities between the United States and the
Central Powers in World War I and between the United
States and Japan and Germany in World War II, who has
been honorably discharged , is a resident voter of the city
and who is an applicant for any position of civil service of
that city shall be entitled to five per cent increase on his ex-
amination mark.
Approved March 26 , 1948 .

(S. B. 89 )
AN ACT relating to redevelopment corporations in cities of the second
class , and the clearing , replanning , rehabilitating and reconstruc-
tion of substandard , insanitary , slum and blighted areas in such
cities, through such corporations and by other means .

Any city of the second class , and its appropriate officers ,
boards or commissions , shall have all of the rights , powers ,
privileges , duties and obligations that are given to cities of
the first class by KRS 99.010 to 99.310 , and all things that
are authorized to be done in , by or for cities of the first class ,
by those sections , may likewise be done in , by or for cities of
the second class , subject to the same terms , conditions and
restrictions . For this purpose , any power or duties vested
in a named officer , board or commission of a city of the first
class , by those sections , shall be deemed to be vested in the
corresponding officer , board or commission of cities of the
second class .
Approved March 23 , 1948 .

court to faithfully discharge their duty. If any commissioner

CHAPTER 158
(S. B. 92 )
AN ACT relating to bonds given in proceedings under Section 489 of
the Civil Code of Practice .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
A new section of the Civil Code of Practice is created ,
to be numbered Section 489a and to read as follows :
Any bond provided for in Section 489 of the Civil Code
shall remain in full force and effect until the guardian or
committee has filed in the county court of the county of his
appointment and qualification a settlement of his accounts
showing payment to , or for the benefit of, his ward , of the
funds secured by such bond , and until the settlement has
been approved and confirmed by the county court . Upon
production to the circuit court in which the bond was ex-
ecuted , of a certified copy of the settlement and of the order
of the county court approving and confirming the same ,
the circuit court shall enter an order releasing the guardian
or committee , and his sureties , from further liability under
the bond .
Approved March 1 , 1948 .

Upon presentation of a certified copy of the settlement and the court order to the circuit court where the bond was executed, the circuit court will release the guardian or committee and their sureties from further liability.

CHAPTER 159
(S. B. 98 )
AN ACT amending KRS 215.320, relating to state aid for acquisition
and operation of district tuberculosis sanitoria .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 215.320 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is
amended to read as follows :
(1 ) Subject to the provisions of subsection ( 3 ) of this
section , every tuberculosis sanatorium district within the
provisions of KRS 215.080 to 215.350 , and every tubercu-
losis sanatorium district established under the provisions of
Chapter 111 of the Acts of 1912 or Chapter 65 of the Acts of
1918 , and amendments thereof, shall be entitled to receive
from funds appropriated for that purpose :
(a) Semiannually, on the first day of January and
July of each year , a sum equal to ten percent of the amount
actually paid and expended by the district during the pre-
ceding six months in acquiring , constructing and equipping
a sanatorium for the care and treatment of tuberculosis
patients and in making improvements , betterments and addi-
tions thereto .
( b) Quarterly , on the first day of January , April , July
and October of each year , two dollars per day for each free
tuberculosis patient treated and cared for in the sanatorium
during the three calendar months next preceding those dates .
(2) Upon the district complying with the provisions of
KRS 215.080 to 215.350 , the Department of Finance shall
draw its warrant upon the State Treasurer in favor of the
district board of trustees for such sum .
(3) The aid rendered by the state under the provisions
of this section shall not in the aggregate exceed one hundred
thousand dollars per year for each sanatorium .
Approved March 23 , 1948 .

CHAPTER 160
(S. B. 105 )
AN ACT relating to research in the fields of medicine and surgery ;
creating a Medical Research Commission with authority to pro-
vide for a research program ; and making appropriations for the
purpose .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
1. There is hereby created a Medical Research Com-
mission to be composed of five members . The Commissioner
of Health shall be ex officio a member and secretary of the
commission . The other four members shall be appointed by
the Governor . Three of the appointed members shall be
physicians licensed to practice in this state , who shall be ap-
pointed by the Governor from a list of three names for each
of the three positions submitted by the Kentucky State Medi-
cal Association . The other appointed member shall be a lay-
man who has evinced an interest in the improvement of medi-
cal services in Kentucky . Each of the appointed members
shall serve for a term of four years and until his successor
is appointed and has qualified . Appointments of successors
and appointments to fill vacancies shall be made from lists
as in the case of original appointments . The members shall
annually select from their own number a chairman , who shall
serve for a term of one year . The members shall serve with-
out compensation but shall be reimbursed for their actual and
necessary traveling expenses , out of sums appropriated to
the commission .
2. The Medical Research Commission shall have authori-
ty to provide for a program of research in the fields of
medicine , surgery, and allied sciences , including technical
and scientific developments in those fields , preventive medi-
cine , control of diseases , educational methods ( including
demonstrations and practical applications of theories of edu-
cation ) , and all other features of medicine and surgery that
affect the health and welfare of the people of this Common-
wealth . The program may be provided for by contract with
any recognized and accredited school of medicine or surgery ,
or research foundation , that is equipped or may be equipped
"

Section 2. Functions .
The Department shall exercise all functions of the State in
relation to :
(a ) Administration and supervision of state unemploy-
ment insurance .
(b) Administration and supervision of state employ-
ment offices .
(c) Administration and supervision of all forms of
public assistance including general home relief , outdoor and
indoor care for persons in need , old age assistance , aid to de-
pendent children , the blind and otherwise handicapped .
(d ) Administration and supervision of all child wel-
fare activities , except those vested in the Department of
Health .
The functions heretofore performed by the Department
of Industrial Relations and the Department of Welfare in
respect of the functions above outlined are hereby transferred
to and vested in the Department of Economic Security . All
administrative functions of the nature outlined above , here-
tofore performed by other agencies of the State , are hereby
abolished and shall hereafter be performed by the Depart-
ment of Economic Security .
Section 3. Bureaus and Divisions .
The Department shall be divided into the following ad-
ministrative bureaus and divisions :
(a) A Bureau of Employment Security which shall be
divided into :
( 1 ) A Division of Unemployment Insurance ; and
(2) A Division of Employment Service .

classification.title: An Act relating to the regulation of hatcheries and dealers in baby chicks and other poultry.
classification.summary: This act requires persons, firms, or corporations operating public hatcheries or dealing in baby chicks, turkey poults, other newly hatched domestic poultry, and hatching eggs to obtain an annual permit from the Livestock Sanitary Division and pay a $10.00 fee. Exemptions are provided for hatcheries, chick dealers, salesmen, or corporations selling less than 1000 chicks per year, or egg dealers selling less than 3500 eggs per year. Violations are considered misdemeanors punishable by a fine up to $100.00. Permits can be suspended or canceled after a hearing, with appeals to the Circuit Court of Franklin County. The Livestock Sanitary Division may confiscate non-compliant products. The permit year runs from July 1 to June 30. All collected funds are to be used for enforcing the act. An emergency is declared due to the danger to public health and the poultry industry from uncontrolled hatching, sale, and importing of poultry and hatching eggs.

SECTION 1. DEFINITION OF THE PRACTICE OF

The practice of clinical psychology is defined as follows :

SECTON 2. BOARD OF EXAMINERS .
On or before July 1 , 1948 ,the Governor shall appoint
from a list of ten submitted to him by the Kentucky Psycho-
logical Association five psychologists holding the degree of
Doctor of Philosophy or its equivalent in psychology from
an accredited educational institution and eligible for licensing
under the provisions of this Act to be members of the board
of examiners of psychologists , one to hold office for one year,
one for two years , one for three years , and two for four years
from said July first . Annually thereafter , as the terms of
the members expire , the Governor shall appoint successors ,
for terms of four years and until their successors are ap-
pointed and qualified . Each appointment shall be from a list
of three names for each position to be filled , submitted by the
Kentucky Psychological Association , and each appointee shall
be a psychologist certified according to the provisions of this
Act . Each member , including the members first appointed ,
shall be an elector of this state and shall have engaged in the
practice or teaching of clinical psychology for at least three
years in this state or elsewhere in the United States . An-
nually the board shall elect one of its members as chairman .
The Governor shall fill any vacancy occurring in the board
with a certified clinical psychologist from a list of three sub-
mitted by the Kentucky Psychological Association for the
unexpired portion of the term . Upon recommendation by a
majority of the board members , and after notice and a hear-
ing by them , the Governor shall remove any member for in-
competence , neglect of duty or malfeasance in office . Three
members of the board shall be a quorum. Each member shall
receive his necessary expenses incurred in the performance
of the duties required by this Act . The board may employ
necessary personnel for the performance of its functions ,
determine the classifications and functions of such personnel ,

CHAPTER 169 383
tory to the board in the practice of clinical psychology ; and

nation given by the board . The board shall grade the
examinations returned by the candidates and shall keep them
for at least one year . Any unsuccessful candidate may , upon
written request to the board, see his graded paper .
SECTION 6. CERTIFICATION UNDER SPECIAL
CONDITIONS .
The board may waive either an examination or the re-
quirements of subsection ( d ) of section 5 if it deems such
action to be in the public interest with respect to any person
who applies within two years from July 1 , 1948 , who meets
the requirements of subsections ( a ) , ( b ) , and ( c ) of section 5 ,
who is qualified by experience to practice clinical psychology
and who has received a Master's Degree or its equivalent in
psychology from any educational institution rated as accept-
able as provided in section 4 and has been engaged in such
practice at least three years at the time of the effective date
of this Act . The board may grant a certificate without
examination to any person who at the time of application is
licensed or certified by a similar board of another state
whose standards , in the opinion of the board , are not lower
than those required by this Act. A fee of twenty -five dollars
shall be paid for each certificate issued under this section .
SECTON 7. RENEWAL FEES .
By June 1 of each year , every certificate holder shall
pay to the board of examiners a renewal fee of three dollars ,
and shall receive a renewal certificate . Any certificate shall
be cancelled if the holder fails to secure the renewal certi-
ficate within three months after the renewal date , but any
certificate so cancelled may be restored by the board upon
payment of a fee of ten dollars within one year after can-
cellation .
SECTION 8. SUSPENSION OR REVOCATION OF
CERTIFICATE .
The board may suspend any such certificate for a definite
period not to exceed one year , or may revoke the same , or

may refuse to issue or renew a certificate , if the person has
been convicted of a felony , has been found by the board to
have employed fraud or deceit in obtaining the certificate , is
guilty of habitual drunkenness or habitual addiction to the
use of habit forming drugs , or has acted negligently or
wrongfully in the conduct of his profession . After three
years from the date of a revocation , an application for re-
instatement may be made to the board , and it may , upon fav-
orable action by the majority of its members , grant such
reinstatement .
SECTION 9. HEARING ON REFUSAL , REVOCA-
TION , SUSPENSION OF LICENSE .
.( 1 ) The board may not suspend or revoke or refuse to
issue or to renew any certificate for any cause listed in sec-
tion 8 , unless the person accused has been given at least
twenty days ' notice in writing of the charge against him and
a public hearing by the board . The written notice shall be
mailed to the person's last known address , but the non-
appearance of the person shall not prevent such a hearing.
( 2 ) Upon such a hearing the board may administer oath
and procure by its subpoenas the attendance of witnesses and
the production of relevant books and papers .
SECTION 10. APPEAL FROM THE BOARD'S RUL-
ING ON CERTIFICATES .
Any person whose certificate has been revoked or sus-
pended by the board may appeal to the Franklin Circuit
Court for a trial de novo by filing with the clerk a certified
copy of the charge heard by the board and his petition re-
questing a trial . The Board shall make certified copies of
any changes upon demand of the applicant . When the copy
of the charge is lodged with the clerk of the Franklin Circuit
Court and the required deposit of court cost is paid within
ten days after the board's findings , the appeal shall be con-
sidered perfected and shall be docketed and stand for trial .
No such appeal shall operate as a supersedeas to such revoca-
tion or suspension .

CHAPTER 170 385
SECTION 11. PRIVILEGED COMMUNICATIONS .
For the purpose of this chapter , the confidential rela-
tions and communications between certified clinical psy-
chologist and client are placed upon the same basis as those
provided by law between attorney and client , and nothing in
this chapter shall be construed to require any such privileged
communication to be disclosed .
SECTION 12. USE OF TITLE .
No person shall use the title " Certified Clinical Psy-
chologist " in this state without a certificate granted by said
board of examiners and signed by each member thereof . No
person not certified as provided in this Act shall designate
himself or his occupation by the words " certified clinical
psychologist " , or by any other term which implies that he
is a clinical psychologist , nor shall any such person engage
in any practice , for remuneration , that would convey to any
person or persons the idea that he is a clinical psychologist
and licensed to operate as such .
SECTION 13. PENALTIES .
Any person who violates any of the provisions of section
12 of this Act shall be fined not less than $100.00 nor more
than $ 500.00 .
SECTION 14. REVOLVING FUND TO BE ESTAB-
LISHED .
All fees received by the board of examiners under this
Act shall be placed in the State Treasury to the credit of a
revolving fund for the use of the board in carrying out its
functions .

CHAPTER 171
(S. B. 147)
AN ACT relating to safety glass in motor vehicles .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Section 189.120 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) On and after January 1 , 1949 , no person shall sell
any new motor vehicle in this state nor shall any new motor
vehicle be registered in this state unless the vehicle is equip-
ped with safety glass wherever glass is used in the windshield ,
doors or windows .
(2) As used in this section , "safety glass " means any
product composed of glass , so manufactured , fabricated , or
treated as substantially to prevent shattering and flying of
the glass when struck or broken , or such other or similar
products as may be approved by the Department of Revenue .
(3 ) The Department of Revenue shall compile and pub-
lish a list of types of glass by name approved by the depart-
ment as meeting the requirements of this section , and shall
cause a copy of the list to be furnished to each county court
clerk . No motor vehicle which is subject to the provisions of
this section shall be registered unless it is equipped with an
approved type of safety glass as required by this section , and

CHAPTER 175
(S. B. 157 )
AN ACT relating to persons who are, or who are alleged or believed
to be, mentally incompetent ; including provisions as to observa-
tion , treatment , hospitalization , inquests, care , custody , and ex-
amination .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Section 202.120 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
(1) If the certificate of the examiners mentioned in
KRS 202.110 states in substance that in the opinion of such
examiners the mental condition of the person is so abnormal
that the safety of the person or of the public requires that
he be committed for a period of care and further medical ex-
amination and observation of his mental condition , the court
may order such person to a state hospital for the insane ,
or to a city or county hospital in the county having a psycho-
pathic ward or facilities suitable , in the court's opinion , for
the care and medical examination and observation of mental
diseases , or , upon receipt of a certificate in writing from the
United States Veterans Administration or other agency of
the United States Government that facilities are available
and that such person is eligible for care or treatment therein ,
to the said United States Veterans Administration or other
agency of the United States Government , for a period of care

(2) Such patient shall not be detained against his will
for more than ten days from the date that he notifies the
superintendent in writing of his desire to leave the hospital ,
except as hereinafter provided in this section . If the super-

for an inquest concerning the sanity of the patient as pro-
vided in KRS Chapter 202 ; however , the court, instead of
appointing examiners as provided in KRS 202.100 and 202.-
110 , shall admit as evidence the affidavit of the superintend-
ent , and clinical director or a staff physician acting in the
latter capacity , of the hospital in which the patient has been
confined , that the defendant is insane ( psychotic ) . If such a
petition is filed , the superintendent of the hospital may con-
tinue to hold the patient, even beyond the expiration of the
ten -day period referred to in this section , until officially
notified by the court that the inquest has been held and the
person has been found to be not insane (not psychotic ) ..
§ 3. Section 203.030 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
(1) The superintendent of any state mental hospital ,
except those designated for the care of the criminal insane ,
shall , when requested by a health officer , receive and care for
any person who needs immediate care and treatment be-
cause of mental derangement other than drug addiction or
drunkenness . Such patient shall be received for a period not
exceeding ten days from and inclusive of the date of request .
The request for admission shall be based on a personal ex-
amination made within three days of the date of the re-
quest , and shall be filed at the institution at the time of the
patient's reception .

classification.title: Chapter 175, Section 397 and Section 203.030 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
classification.summary: This statute outlines the procedures for admitting and detaining individuals in state mental hospitals. It details the conditions under which a superintendent can receive and care for an applicant, the process for involuntary detention beyond ten days (requiring a petition for an inquest concerning sanity), and the admission of patients for immediate care due to mental derangement (excluding drug addiction or drunkenness) for a period not exceeding ten days. It also specifies that the superintendent of state mental hospitals, except those for the criminal insane, shall admit patients upon request from a health officer.

CHAPTER 176 399
(3) " Chiropody " means the diagnosis , and the local ,
medical , mechanical and surgical treatment , of ailments of the
human foot , and massage in connection therewith , except am-
putation of the foot or toes , or the use of anaesthetics other
than local, or the use of drugs or medicine other than local ;
(4) "Chiropodist " means one who practices chiropody.
§ 2. Scope and Application of Act .
This act shall not apply to :
( 1 ) Any person manufacturing or selling, as merchan-
dise in a duly established mercantile establishment , shoes or
appliances designed and intended to equalize pressure on
different parts of the foot , or the sale by any licensed drug-
gist of plasters , salves , and lotions for corns , warts , callosites
and bunions , provided such persons shall make no diagnosis
or recommendation and shall prescribe no remedy or treat-
ment ;
( 2) Or interfere in any manner with the practice of any
person whose religion treats or administers to the sick or
suffering by purely spiritual means , nor with any individual's
selection of any such person ;
(3) Physicians licensed by the state board of medical
examiners of this state ;
(4 ) Surgeons of the United States army, navy, United
States Veterans Administration , and United States Public
Health Service , when in actual performance of their official
duties .
$ 3. License Required ..
No person shall profess to be a chiropodist , or practice
or assume the duties incident to chiropody in this state , or
use the title " chiropodist " or any words or letters which
designate or tend to designate to the public that the person
is a chiropodist , unless he holds a valid , current license from
the Board , registered in the county in which the holder is en-
gaged in such practice . Any person who violates this section
shall forfeit any right to compensation for services rendered .

§ 4. Official Examiners .
( 1 ) There shall be a committee of five official examin-
ers in Chiropody , who shall be appointed by the President of
the Board , and whose function it shall be to examine ap-
plicants for license to practice chiropody . Two members shall
be appointed from members of the Board , and each of the
remaining three members shall be a licensed chiropodist , a
citizen of the United States and a resident of this state and
shall have been engaged in the practice of chiropody in this
state for at least five years . The three chiropodist members
shall be appointed for terms of three years , and until their
successors are appointed and qualified , except that upon the
appointment of the first members , one shall be designated to
serve for one year, one for two years , and one for three years .
The two members appointed from the Board shall be appoint-
ed for terms of one year . All appointments of chiropodist
members , including appointments to fill vacancies , shall be
made from lists of three names for each position submitted
by the Kentucky Association of Chiropodists . All vacancies
shall be filled by the President of the Board .
(2 ) No member of the official examiners shall be a
stockholder , officer , member of the faculty or board of trus-
tees of any school , college or institution of chiropody .
( 3 ) The official examiners shall have the power , subject
to the approval of the President of the Board , to make such
rules and regulations , not inconsistent with this Act , as may
be necessary in the performance of its duties .
§ 5. Examinations ; issuance of licenses .
( 1 ) The official examiners shall hold examinations of
applicants for licenses to practice chiropody in this state
semi -annually at such times and places as the Board shall
fix , and shall admit to examination any applicant who :
(a) Is at least twenty-one years of age ;
..(b) Is a citizen of the United States and a resident of
the State of Kentucky ;
(c ) Is of good moral character and temperate habits ;
and

§ 8. Expiration Date of License ; Renewal; Fee .
( 1 ) Licenses granted under this Act shall expire on
June 30 following the date of issuance unless sooner revoked
and cancelled .
(2 ) Licenses may be renewed annually by filing an ap-
plication for renewal with the Board prior to July 1 of each
year, upon blanks prescribed by the Board , and paying a re-
newal fee of Five Dollars . The Board shall annually send
notices to all licensed choropodists in this state prior to this
date .
§ 9. Chiropodist to Practice under Own Name; Title
Used .
(1 ) No person shall offer to practice chiropody under
the name of any company . Any person practicing or offering
to practice choropody shall practice under his own name
only, as his name appears in his license .
(2) No person practicing or offering to practice chi-
ropody shall use any title or abbreviation thereof except
the designation " Chiropodist " . The term " Foot Specialist "
may be used in conjunction with the title " Chiropodist , " but
not as a substitute therefor and not alone .
§ 10. Chiropodist to Display Licenses ; Advertising .
(1 ) Any person who practices chiropody shall display
his license , together with the annual license renewal certifi-
cate , in a conspicuous place in the office in which he practices .
(2 ) No person practicing or offering to practice chi-
ropody shall in any manner advertise the prices charged for
his services ; nor shall he advertise in newspapers , in period-
icals , by handbills or circulars , or in bold -face type in any
printed matter , or by the use of any form of display sign ;
except that he may , upon opening an office or upon change
of address , mail or publish announcements to that effect pro-
vided such announcements shall not appear more than three

§ 11. Grounds for Denial , Revocation or Suspension
of License.
The Board may refuse to license or renew , or may sus-
pend or revoke the license of any chiropodist , upon proof
that he :
( 1 ) Has been convicted of a felony ;
( 2) Has been guilty of moral turpitude or conduct like-
ly to deceive or defraud the public ;
( 3 ) Has employed , hired , procured or induced a person
not licensed to practice chiropody in this state to so practice ;
( 4) Has aided or abetted in the practice of chiropody a
person not licensed to practice chiropody in this state ;
(5 ) Has been granted a license upon a mistake of a
material fact ; or
( 6 ) Has violated any provision of this Act .
§ 12. Proceedings to Revoke , Suspend or Refuse to
Renew License .
( 1 ) A suspension or revocation of a license , or a refusal
to renew a license , may be made by the Board only after a
hearing of which the accused has been given adequate notice .
At the hearing , the Board and the accused shall have the
benefit of counsel .
(2 ) The Board may administer oaths , take the deposi-
tions of witnesses as in civil cases and compel them to attend
before it in person as in civil cases by subpoena issued over
the signature of the President of the Board and in the name
of the state .
$ 13. Record of Revocation or Suspension .
The revocation or suspension of any license shall be noted
upon the records of the Board , and a revoked license shall be
marked as cancelled upon the date of its revocation . The
Board shall, upon entry of an order of suspension or revoca--
tion, transmit to the county clerk of the county in which the
license affected by the order is recorded , a certified copy of

§ 14. Appeal from Order of Revocation or Suspension.
A licensee whose license has been revoked or suspended
by the Board , or who has been refused a renewal license , may ,
within thirty days of the entry of the order , appeal to the
circuit court of the county in which the hearing was held . The
person taking the appeal shall serve upon the Board a written
notice of the appeal , and shall file with the Board an appeal
bond with good and sufficient surety , to be approved by the
President of the Board , conditioned for the speedy prosecu-
tion of the appeal and the payment of any costs charged
against him upon the appeal . The Board , within ten days
after the service of notice of appeal and the filing and ap-
proval of the bond , shall transmit to the clerk of the court a
certified copy , under the seal of the Board , of the accusations
on which the ruling of the Board was based and the ground
for appeal , together with the notice and bond of appeal . The
clerk shall then docket the case , and it shall be tried in all
respects like an ordinary civil action . Either party may ap-
peal from the judgment of the circuit court to the Court of
Appeals as in a civil action . The order appealed from shall
be stayed from the date of the approval of the bond until
final determination of the appeal .
§ 15. Penalties .
Any person who shall present to the county clerk for the
purpose of registration any license which has been fradu-
lently obtained , or shall obtain any license under this Act by
false or fraudulent statement or representation , or shall
practice chiropody under a false or assumed name or shall
falsely impersonate another practitioner or former practi-
tioner of a like or different name , or shall aid and abet any
person in the practice of chiropody within the state without
conforming to the requirements of this Act , or shall otherwise
violate or neglect to comply with any of the provisions of this
Act, shall be fined not less than one hundred dollars nor more
than two hundred dollars , or imprisoned in the county jail
for a term of not less than three months nor more than one

It specifies fines and imprisonment for individuals who present fraudulent licenses, obtain licenses through false statements, practice under false pretenses, impersonate others, or aid unlicensed individuals. The act also establishes an appeal process to the circuit court for licensees whose licenses are revoked, suspended, or denied renewal.

CHAPTER 177
(S. B. 160)
AN ACT Repealing and Re -enacting Section 117.210 Kentucky Re-
vised Statutes , 1946 Edition .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
That Section 117.210 Kentucky Revised Statutes , 1946
Edition be , and the same is , hereby repealed and re -enacted
so that as re -enacted said Section shall read as follows :
" 117.210 ( 1486b -34 ) Employes of board . The board of
registration commissioners shall appoint a registrar and an
assistant registrar who shall act as respectively secretary and
assistant secretary of the board. They shall have general
charge of its office , records and employes under the super-
vision and direction of the board and shall perform the duties
that the board prescribes . They shall hold office at the pleasure
of the board and devote their entire time to the duties of their
office . They shall be members of opposite political parties ,
each member of the board having the right to designate the
one to be chosen from the political party of which he is a
member . The board may employ clerks and employes for such
periods of time as it deems necessary for the proper conduct
of its business . The employes of the board shall be employed
as nearly as practicable in equal numbers from the two
political parties represented on the board . The board may
require bond with security from the registrar or any employe ,
and may fix the terms and penalty of the bond . The registrar
and assistant registrar shall each receive the same annual
entries.citation: Acts of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky, 1948, p. 420

classification.title: AN ACT Repealing and Re-Enacting Section 117.210 Kentucky Revised Statutes, 1946 Edition
classification.summary: This act repeals and re-enacts Section 117.210 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes, 1946 Edition. The re-enacted section details the appointment and duties of a registrar and assistant registrar for the board of registration commissioners. It specifies that these individuals will manage the office, records, and employees under the board's supervision. They are to devote their entire time to their duties and hold office at the pleasure of the board. A key provision states that the registrar and assistant registrar must be members of opposite political parties, with each board member having the right to designate one appointee from their party. The board can also employ clerks and other employees for necessary periods and may require bonds from them. The statute also mandates that employees be hired in roughly equal numbers from the two political parties represented on the board.
classification.reasoning: The statute concerns the administrative structure and employment practices of a board of registration commissioners. It focuses on political party affiliation for appointing registrar and assistant registrar positions and for hiring general employees. There is no mention of race, nor are there any provisions that historically would have been used to enforce racial segregation or discrimination. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law.

CHAPTER 178
(S. B. 164 )
AN ACT relating to the establishment of group plan insurance for em-
ployes of cities and their boards and commissions .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
The legislative body of any city and boards and com-
missions appointed by the legislative body are authorized to
form their employes into a group , or groups , for the purpose
of their obtaining the advantages of the group plan of life
insurance, to aid such employes as long as they continue
under such employment by paying from the funds of the city
or from the operating expense fund of such board or com-
mission any sum annually not exceeding fifty percent of the
annual premiums on such policies , to deduct from the salaries
of said employees that part of the premiums which are to be
paid by them and to contract with the insurer on such terms
as may be provided for in such policies of insurance .
Not Approved .
Received by Secretary of State March 30, 1948 .

CHAPTER 180 407
Section 1. The Clerk of the State Fiscal Court , to -wit :
The Clerk of the Franklin Circuit Court , is authorized to
make requisition upon the Division of Purchases of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky for the necessary office equipment ,
files and supplies for the accommodation and preservation of
suits and records in Fiscal Cases filed in the Franklin Circuit
Court by and against the Commonwealth of Kentucky and the
departments thereof .
Section 2. It shall be the duty of the Director of the
Division of Purchases to procure said supplies and equip-
ment ordered and forward them to the Clerk of the Franklin
Circuit Court when such order and requisition has been
authorized and approved by the Judge of the Franklin Circuit
Court .
Section 3. The Director of the Division of Purchases
shall certify the costs of said supplies and equipment to the
Division of Finance of the Commonwealth of Kentucky for
payment , and it shall be the duty of the Division of Finance
to pay for said equipment and supplies not to exceed $300.00
per annum out of the general fund revenue not appropriated
for other purposes.
Approved March 25 , 1948 .

classification.title: Amendment to Kentucky Revised Statutes Section 76.080 and Creation of Section 76.091
classification.summary: This statute amends Kentucky Revised Statutes Section 76.080, subsections (7) and (10), to clarify the powers of a metropolitan sewer district regarding by-laws, agreements, property use, and the establishment and collection of sewer rates. It specifically states that sewer rates applicable within a city of the first class are subject to the approval, supervision, and control of the city's legislative body. A new section, 76.091, is created, granting the legislative body of a city of the first class "visitorial powers" over the sewer district, allowing them to make inquiries, examinations, call for reports, and effect changes in sewer rates through ordinances after investigation and public hearings.
classification.reasoning: The statute deals with the administrative and financial regulation of a metropolitan sewer district within a city of the first class in Kentucky. It outlines the powers of the sewer district's board and the oversight role of the city's legislative body concerning sewer rates and operations. There is no mention of race, nor are there any provisions that implicitly suggest racial discrimination. The focus is purely on municipal governance and utility management. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law.

CHAPTER 181 411
Section 1. Definitions . As used in this Act, unless the
context requires otherwise :
(a) "Board " means the Kentucky Board of Veterinary
Examiners .
(b) " Commissioner " means the Commissioner of Agri-
culture , Labor and Statistics .
(c) " Animal " means any animal except human beings .
(d) "Compensation " includes any gift, bonus , fee ,
money , credit or other thing of value .
(e) " Veterarian " means a practitioner of veterinary
medicine who is duly licensed in the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky .
(f) " Practicing veterinary medicine " except as herein-
after stated , means : A person shall be considered as practic-
ing veterinary medicine who holds himself out as being able
to diagnose , treat , operate on, or prescribe for any animal
disease , pain , injury , deformity or physical condition and who
shall either offer or undertake by any means or methods to
diagnose , treat , operate on or prescribe for any animal pain ,
injury , deformity or physical condition for compensation ; or
who for compensation or with expectation of receiving com-
pensation , advises , recommends , administers or dispenses a
drug , medicine , appliance or application , operation or treat-
ment of whatever nature for the prevention , cure or relief of
a wound , fracture or bodily injury or disease of any animal ;
or any person who shall advertise or hold himself out to the
public as a veterinary physician , surgeon , or specialist or who
shall use any title so indicating or who shall append to his
name either by prefix, affix , or otherwise , the letters V. S. ,
D. V. S. , M. D. V. , D. V. M. , V. M. D. or B. V. Sc.
Section 2. There hereby is created a board to be known
as the " Kentucky Board of Veterinary Examiners ." Said
Board shall consist of the Commissioner , who shall be Chair-
man , and four (4 ) appointed members . A person to be eligible
for appointment as a member of said Board shall be a citizen
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky who shall have been law-
fully engaged in the practice of veterinary medicine in this
Commonwealth for at least five ( 5 ) years next preceding the

erinarian's license issued to him by the proper authority of
any other State of the United States or any Country , if the
requirements of such State or Country under which the said
license was issued , at the time of the issuance of such license ,
were of a standard not lower than the standards required for
the issuance of a new license under the provisions of this Act
and if such other State or Country recognizes and accepts the
veterinarian's licenses issued under the provisions of this Act.
The Board may make all rules and regulations, not incon-
sistent with this Act, governing the matter of reciprocity
with other States and Countries , and among other things may
require that such applicant submit proof , satisfactory to the
Board , of his good moral character and fitness , and that such
applicant has been actively engaged in the practice of vet-
erinary medicine for such period of time as may appear to
the Board to be reasonable , not less , however , than a period
of one year next preceding the date of such application .
Section 8. Any person not entitled to a license or to a
certificate of license under the provisions of Sections 5 and 7
of this Act , or any person who hereafter shall wish to practice
veterinary medicine in this Commonwealth , before entering
upon the practice of veterinary medicine shall first pass an
examination as to his qualifications and fitness to engage in
such practice . Said examinations shall be conducted by the
Board . An applicant for such examination shall be a graduate
of a veterinary college having standards and requirements
equal to the standards and requirements adopted by the
American Veterinary Medical Association in 1941. He shall
be at least twenty-one years of age and shall be of good moral
character .
An application to the Board for admission to a licensing
examination shall be filed with the Secretary-Treasurer of the

Each person who successfully passes an examination be-
fore the Board shall receive a certificate of license to practice

Section 9. Each examination hereinabove provided for
shall be held by the Board upon the following subjects :
(1 ) The subjects of the written examination shall be :

(b) Physiology and biochemistry .

cology .

( 5 ) Non -licensed veterinarians and others in the United

while engaged in the performance of their official duties , or
other lawfully qualified veterinarians residing in other States ,

or any lawfully qualified veterinarian residing near the border

State to practice veterinary medicine in that State whose
practice extends into the limits of this State ; but such prac-
titioner shall not open any office or appoint any place to meet
clients or prospective clients within the limits of this State .
(6 ) Nothing in this Act shall prevent any person from
treating diseases of animals for compensation at any place
which is twenty ( 20 ) miles or more distant from the office or
place of business of the nearest practicing veterinarian who
is licensed as such under the laws of this Commonwealth .
Nothing in this chapter shall interfere with the profes-
sional activities of any licensed pharmacist or prevent him
from selling drugs , medicines , appliances or any other product
used in the prevention or treatment of swine diseases .

Section 17. The Board may refuse to issue a certificate
of license provided for in this Act for any of the following
causes :
(1 ) The presentation to the Board of any license , cer-
tificate or diploma which was illegally or fraudulently ob-
tained , or for any fraudulent conduct of the party in passing
an examination given under the provisions of this Act.
( 2 ) Habitual drunkenness or habitual addiction to the

Section 18. The Board may suspend or revoke a certifi-
cate of license or license for any of the causes for which it
may refuse to grant a license under the provisions of this
Act , and for the following causes :
(1 ) For dishonesty in applying a tuberculin test on
cattle or making a false record of such test .
(2 ) For dishonesty in applying a brucellosis (Bang's
disease ) test or making a false record of such test .
(3 ) For the procuring of a license by fraudulent means .
(4 ) For false advertising of medical practice intended ,
or having a tendency , to deceive and defraud the public .
(5 ) For the conviction of any felony or crime involving
moral turpitude .
(6) Any gross negligence , incompetence or misconduct
in the practice of veterinary medicine in this Commonwealth .

CHAPTER 181 423
of such applicant or licensee . The hearing on such charges
shall be at such time and place as the Board shall prescribe .
Section 20. In the preparation and conduct of such hear-
ings the Board shall have the power to require by subpoena
the attendance and testimony of witnesses and the production
of papers and records , and any member of the Board , when
authorized by the Board , may sign and issue subpoenas ,
administer oaths and affirmations , examine witnesses and hear
and receive evidence . The fees and mileage shall be the same
as prescribed by law in judicial procedure in the courts in
this State in civil cases . Any party to any hearing before the
Board shall have the right to the attendance of witnesses in
his behalf at such hearing upon making a request thereof to
the Board and designating the person or persons sought to
be subpoened and their location or addresses .
In case of disobedience to the subpoena the Board may
invoke the aid of any court of competent jurisdiction in
requiring the attendance and testimony of witnesses and the
production of records and papers ; and such court may issue
an order requiring the persons to appear before the Board
and give evidence and to produce records and papers , as the
case may be ; and any failure to obey such order may be
punished by the court as contempt thereof .
Testimony may be taken by deposition as in civil cases
and any person may be compelled to appear and to depose in
the same manner as witnesses may be compelled to appear
and testify as hereinabove provided .
Any person who shall neglect or refuse to attend and
testify or to answer any lawful inquiry or to produce docu-
mentary evidence if in his power to do so , in obedience to a
subpoena or lawful requirement by said Board or member
thereof, shall be guilty of a misdemeanor and upon conviction
thereof by a court of competent jurisdiction shall be fined not
less than Ten Dollars ($10.00 ) nor more than Fifty Dollars
($50.00 ) or imprisoned not less than ten ( 10) days nor more
than thirty ( 30 ) days , or both so fined and imprisoned in the
discretion of the jury.

the order of the Board on the ground that it is unlawful or

The answer of the Board shall be filed within twenty ( 20 )

The action will then be at issue , and stand ready for trial

Section 24. If an action is commenced against the Board ,
as authorized in Section 23 of this Act , the Board shall
promptly cause a certified transcript of all proceedings had
and testimony taken therein to be made and filed with the
Clerk of the Court in which the action is pending . The cost
of preparing and certifying the record shall be paid to the
Board by the party bringing the action , and taxed as part of
the costs , to be paid as directed by the Court upon final deter-
mination of the action . Copies of the transcript shall be fur-
nished at cost to any party to the proceeding . The action or
appeal shall be determined by the Court upon the record
certified and filed as herein provided , and no new or additional
evidence shall be heard or considered by the Court unless it
appears to the Court from said record that the complaining
party was denied the opportunity of a fair hearing before the
Board .
The Court shall enter a judgment either sustaining the
order of the Board , or setting it aside , or vacating it in whole
or in part . Either party to the action may , within sixty ( 60 )
days after the entry of the judgment of the Circuit Court ,
appeal to the Court of Appeals , and the appeal when filed in
the office of the Clerk of the Court of Appeals shall be
docketed and advanced in the same manner as Commonwealth
cases .

426 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Section 25. No person who has had a license revoked for
cause shall be issued another license within five (5 ) years
from the date of such revocation .
Section 26. Any person who shall violate or aid in the
violation of any of the provisions of this Act shall be guilty
of a misdemeanor and upon conviction shall be fined not less
than Ten Dollars ( $ 10.00 ) nor more than Five Hundred Dol-
lars ( $ 500.00 ) , or sentenced to jail for not less than ten ( 10)
nor more than ninety ( 90 ) days , or both so fined and im-
prisoned in the discretion of the jury .
Section 27. The practice of veterinary medicine , veteri-
nary surgery or veterinary dentistry by any unregistered ,
unlicensed person hereby is declared to be a menace and a
nuisance , dangerous to the public health and safety , and it
shall be the duty of the Board to promptly abate or cause
to be abated such practice by instituting or causing to be
instituted in the name of the Commonwealth of Kentucky ,
on relation of said Board , a suit in a court of competent juris-
diction in the county in which such practice is conducted for
an injunction , and upon proof of such violation the court shall
issue an injunction prohibiting such persons from engaging
in the practice of veterinary medicine , veterinary surgery or
veterinary dentistry unless it shall appear to the court that
such practitioner is duly licensed and registered as provided
in this Act. The Board hereby is empowered to file such peti-
tions and prosecute such actions , and it is made its duty so
to do . The liability for or imposition of penalties provided
for in this Act shall not constitute any basis or grounds for
the denial or an injunction to prohibit the practice of veter-
inary medicine , veterinary surgery or veterinary dentistry
without a certificate of license as provided in this Act .
Section 28. Chapter 321 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes hereby is repealed in its entirety ; and all other laws and
parts of laws in conflict herewith hereby are repealed to the
extent of such conflict .
Section 29. The sections , clauses , sentences and parts of
this Act are severable and are not matters of mutual essential
entries.citation: Acts of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky, 1948, p. 441

CHAPTER 182
(S. B. 181 )
AN ACT regulating the propagation and keeping of bees , the suppression
of contagious diseases among bees , providing for the registration
and regulation of apiaries ; and repealing Chapter 252 of the Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. As used in this Act, unless the context re-
quires otherwise ; ( 1 ) " Bees " means any stage of the common
hive or honey bee ( Apis mellifica ) or other bees kept for the
production of honey or wax.
(2 ) " Bee diseases " means American or European foul
brood, sacbrood , bee paralysis , disappearing disease , Isle of
Wight disease , or any other disease or abnormal condition of
the eggs, larval , pupal or adult stage of bees .

Section 2. The commissioner may appoint a competent

apiarists as may be necessary to carry out the provisions of
this Act, and to fix their compensation . The state apiarist
and deputy state apiarist shall be vested with the powers of
police officers in the enforcement of the provisions of this act .

rules and orders as in his judgment may be necessary to con-

control or eradication, without remuneration to the owner .

therefrom to the commissioner within five days of the service

appeal shall suspend the operation of the order appealed from

the commissioner shall be open to the public and his decisions

tingencies , to correct errors , or to avoid cash deficits , the

ments . The Commissioner of Finance shall make quarterly
allotments of appropriations , except in the case of appropria-
tions for the purpose of capital outlay for buildings , roads
and bridges , and for printing , as provided in KRS 45.200 .

shall be classified according to purpose and type , and the

AN ACT relating to condemnation of property , including the right of
ingress and egress to and from such property , for oil or gas pipe
lines and facilities related thereto .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
SECTION 1. Any corporation or partnership organized
for the purpose of, and any individual engaged in or pro-
posing to engage in , constructing , maintaining or operating
oil or gas wells or pipe lines for transporting or delivering
oil or gas in public service may , if it is unable to contract or
agree with the owner after a good faith effort to do so , con-
demn the lands and material or the use and occupation of the
lands that are necessary for constructing , maintaining and
operating its pipe lines and all necessary machinery , pumping
stations , appliances and fixtures , including tanks and tele-
phone and telegraph lines , for use in connection therewith ,
and the necessary rights of ingress and egress to construct ,
examine , alter , repair , maintain , operate or remove such pipe
lines . The proceedings for condemnation shall be as provided
in this Act .
SECTION 2. A corporation , partnership or individual
seeking to condemn lands and material or the use and occupa-
tion of lands , under the provisions of Section 1 of this Act,
may file a verified petition in the office of the county court
clerk of the county in which all or the greater portion of the
land and material is located . The petition shall state that it
is filed under the provisions of this Act and shall contain , in
substance ,
(1 ) Allegations sufficient to show that petitioner is en-
titled , under the provisions of section 1 of this Act, to con-
demn the lands and material or the use and occupation of the
lands in question ;
(2 ) A particular description of the land , the use and
occupation thereof and the material sought to be condemned ;

except where the provisions of this Act specifically or by
