444 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
CHAPTER 187
(S. B. 195 )
AN ACT relating to the payment of state checks, clearance of outdated
state checks from the treasurer's records and providing for the
payment of outdated checks.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Any check issued by the State Treasurer shall not be
valid as an order for payment after one year from its date
of issue and this limitation shall appear on all checks issued .
The State Treasurer is authorized to clear his records of all
such unpaid checks and credit the amounts to the General
Expenditure Fund.
When any properly endorsed check issued by the State
Treasurer is presented for payment more than one year after
its date of issue and within the period prescribed by Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes , Section 413.120 , the State Treasurer
shall, upon satisfactory proof of ownership , cancel such check
and forward it to the Commissioner of Finance with the date
and number of the Pay-In-Voucher , which credited the
amount of such check to the General Expenditure Fund . The
Commissioner of Finance shall draw a warrant against the
check . All endorsements on the surrendered check shall have
the same effect as if the check were paid by the drawee . All
checks paid in the manner herein set forth shall be charged
against the General Expenditure Fund .
Approved March 22, 1948

CHAPTER 189 445
SECTION I.
Section 427.130 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is here-
by amended and re-enacted to read as follows :
( 1 ) Salaries for sums due state , county , city and school
board officers and employees shall be subject to attachment
or garnishment.
( 2 ) All sums due any person from the Commonwealth
of Kentucky , or any agency or department thereof, and all
sums due from any county school board , city or county , shall
be subject to attachment or garnishment .
(3) Service of attachments or garnishments upon the
Commonwealth of Kentucky shall be made by serving the
Commissioner of Finance , and the State Treasurer . No at-
tachment or garnishment served upon the Commonwealth
shall be valid unless the process shall specify the name of the
employee , or other creditor , and the budget unit and division
thereof through which the sum alleged to be due is payable .
SECTION II .
WHEREAS , the present procedure in the service of
attachments and garnishments upon the Commonwealth is
inadequate , inefficient , and uncertain , an emergency is de-
clared to exist and this Act shall take effect upon its passage
and upon approval of the Governor .
Approved March 26 , 1948 .

( a) Protect and promote the health of the general

to medical and surgical hospital facilities and related services .

will aid in providing the facilities and services to meet the
needs found to exist in the State .

behalf of the State any grants , gifts or contributions now or
hereafter made by the Federal Government , or from any other
source , to the Commonwealth of Kentucky to aid and assist
in carrying out the purposes and provisions of Public Law
No. 725 , ( 79th Congress ) Chapter 958 , 2nd Session , or any
other acts for the same or similar purposes .
(d) Convert all funds received from grants , gifts or
contributions into the State Treasury which shall be placed
in a revolving fund to be disbursed on authorization of the

sioner of Finance .

452 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
A Sinking Fund Commission hereby is created , which
shall be composed of the Governor , the State Treasurer , the
Commissioner of Revenue , the Attorney General, and the
Commissioner of Finance . It shall have the authority , but
shall not be required , to invest , in direct obligations of the
United States Government , or direct obligations of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky , any or all sinking funds of the Com-
monwealth , and any or all other funds of the Commonwealth
to the extent that such funds contain surplus sums in excess of
the sums required to meet appropriations or other charges
against such funds , except that such authority shall not extend
to any funds that are specifically provided by law to be in-
vested by some other officer or agency of the State Govern-
ment . It shall have the authority to enter into contracts with
the United States Government , or any department of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky , to carry out the provisions of this
Act. The above authority is limited to this extent only : Any
investment herein authorized shall be one that will empower
the Commission to demand par value payment of the securities
invested in upon ninety ( 90 ) days ' notice .
WHEREAS , there are many millions of dollars of funds
of the Commonwealth that are not bearing interest payable to
the Commonwealth , an emergency is hereby declared to exist ,
and this Act shall become effective upon its passage and ap-
proval by the Governor .
Approved March 13 , 1948 .

transmitting or using electricity ; (6 ) any other machinery or
apparatus connected with or operated by any of the previously
named boilers , vessels or machines ; and including the in-
cidental power to make inspections of and to issue certificates
of inspection upon , any such boilers, apparatus, and
machinery , whether insured or otherwise .
(g) Insurance against loss of or damage to any property
resulting from burglary , theft , larceny, robbery, forgery ,
fraud , vandalism, malicious mischief , confiscation or wrong-
ful conversion , disposal or concealment by any person or per-
sons , or from any attempt at any of the foregoing ; and in-
surance against loss of or damage to moneys , coins , bullion ,
securities , notes , drafts , acceptances or any other valuable
papers or documents , resulting from any cause , except while
in the custody or possession of and being transported by any
carrier for hire or in the mail ; insurance of individuals by
means of an all -risk type of policy commonly known as the
"Personal Property Floater ' against any kind and all kinds
of loss of or damage to , or loss of use of , any personal prop-
erty other than merchandise .
(h) To guarantee and indemnify merchants , traders , and
those engaged in business and giving credit , from loss or
damage by reason of giving and extending credit to their
customers and those dealing with them , and to cause itself
to be insured against any loss or risk it may have incurred
in the course of its business , and upon the interest it may
have in any property by means of any loans that it may have
made on mortgages ; and including incidental power to ac-
quire and dispose of debts so insured and to collect any debts
owned to such insurer or to any person so insured by him .

CHAPTER 197
(S. B. 221 )
AN ACT relating to mutual insurance companies , other than life ; amend-
ing KRS 301.050 and 301.070 and subsection ( 1) of KRS 301.030 .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Sec . 1. Subsection ( 1 ) of Section 301.030 of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) When by the laws of any other state , district or ter-
ritory any company is authorized to engage in the business
of insurance , other than life insurance , on the mutual plan
in accordance with the laws of the state , district or territory
in which the company is organized , and is solvent and pos-
sessed of a surplus over and above all of its liabilities at least
equal to the amount required by KRS 301.050 but not less than
three hundred thousand dollars if authorized to transact any
of the kinds of insurance specified in subsection ( 2 ) of KRS
301.070 , it shall be admitted to do the kinds of insurance per-
mitted to like mutual insurance companies organized under
the laws of the Commonwealth when it shall have complied
with the following requirements ; to wit :
(a ) Filed with the Director a duly certified copy of the
charter and articles of association .

Sec . 2. Section 301.050 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows , to wit :
( 1 ) No such company shall issue policies or transact any
business of insurance until the Director has , by formal license ,
authorized it to do so .
( 2 ) A license shall not be issued to a company organized
to transact the kinds of insurance described in subsection ( 1 )
of KRS 301.070 until the company has complied with the fol-
lowing conditions :
(a ) Applications for at least two hundred risks , for at
least twenty members , shall be subscribed , aggregating not
less than five hundred thousand dollars insurance .
(b ) The maximum of any single risk less reinsurance
shall not exceed ten per cent of the surplus possessed by the
company . Any reinsurance shall take effect simultaneously
with the policy , being deducted in determining the maximum
single risk.
(c) A premium upon each application shall be collected
in cash and the company shall hold a surplus of not less than
fifty thousand dollars .

struction of , or damage to , the property of any other person .
(m) Insurance against any other risk not otherwise
specified in this subsection or in subsection ( 1 ) of this section
and which may lawfully be the subject of insurance but not
including life insurance .
(3) A company authorized to transact the type of in-
surance described in paragraph (i ) of subsection (2 ) of this
section may , without additional surplus , also insure against
loss or damage by fire of or to any automobile .
(4 ) The right to transact more than one of the kinds of
insurance described in subsections ( 1 ) and ( 2 ) of this section
shall be subject to the provisions of KRS 301.050 .
Approved March 25 , 1948

CHAPTER 198
(S. B. 223)
AN ACT relating to motor vehicles , with particular reference to the
granting of operator's licenses.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Notwithstanding the provisions of subsection ( 1 ) of KRS
186.440 , any person between the ages of fourteen and sixteen
may be granted an operator's license for the operation of a
motor vehicle of not more than five horse power .
Approved March 25 , 1948 .
CHAPTER 199
(S. B. 229 )
AN ACT to amend and re-enact Section 230.030 of the Kentucky Re-
vised Statutes , relating to the Secretary of the State Racing Com-
mission .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
That Section 230.030 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes of
1946 be amended and re - enacted so as to read as follows :
(1 ) The Governor shall appoint a secretary of the Com-
mission, for a term of not exceeding four years .
(2 ) The secretary shall keep a full record of the pro-
ceedings of the Commission , preserve at its general office all
books , maps , documents and papers entrusted to the Commis-
sion, and perform such other duties as the Commission pre-
scribes .
(3 ) The secretary shall receive an annual salary , to be
fixed by the Commission , which shall be paid by proportionate
contributions of the several racing associations licensed by the
Commission . The Commission , on or before December 1 of
each year, shall assess upon each racing association its just
proportion of the salary of the secretary .
Approved March 19 , 1948 .

CHAPTER 200
(S. B. 230 )
AN ACT repealing and re -enacting Section 89.560 , Kentucky Revised
Statutes, relating to city managers in cities of the second , third and
fourth classes , and declaring an emergency to exist .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 89.560 , Kentucky Revised Statutes ,
1946 Edition , is hereby amended and re -enacted to read as
follows :
(1 ) The board of commissioners shall select and employ
a city manager . He shall be selected solely on the basis of his
executive and administrative qualifications . The choice shall
not be limited to inhabitants of the city or state . He shall be
employed for an indefinite period , in cities of the second and
fourth classes , or for a period not exceeding four years in
cities of the third class . He shall receive such compensation
as may be determined by the board of commissioners . During
the absence or disability of the city manager the board of com-
missioners shall designate some properly qualified person to
perform the duties of the office .
(2 ) In cities of the second class the city manager shall be
removable at will without cause by a majority of the board of
commissioners .
(3 ) In cities of the third and fourth classes the city
manager shall be removable at will by the board of commis-
sioners , but removal shall not be effected by a reduction in
his compensation . If removed at any time after having served
for six ( 6 ) months he may in writing demand written charges
and a public hearing before the board of commissioners prior
to the date on which his final removal shall take effect , but
during the hearing the board of commissioners may suspend
him from office . The public hearing shall be fixed by the board
of commissioners not to exceed thirty ( 30 ) days after demand
therefor .
Section 2. WHEREAS , there now exists great confusion
and conflict in the administration of the affairs of cities of
the second class having a city manager form of government

CHAPTER 202
(S. B. 234)
AN ACT relating to forest fire protection , forest fire detection , preven-
tion and suppression .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section I. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is hereby created to be known as KRS 149.105 and it
shall read as follows :
Section 149.105 . " Timberland " as used in KRS 149.140
to 149.190 means any land which has enough timber or woody
brush, standing or down , to constitute a fire menace to itself
or adjoining lands , but does not include lands under cultiva-
tion or entirely in grass , nor land that is an isolated fire risk
unless a forest fire on it would imperil the lands of an adjoin-
ing landowner.
Section II. Section 149.110 Kentucky Revised Statutes
is hereby amended to read as follows :

( 1 ) During the seasons of the year when there is danger
of forest fires , every owner of timberland shall provide a suf-

( 2 ) Actual bona fide residence by the owner or lessor

tection for the tract , but such residence shall not relieve the
owner , his agents or employees from responsibility for the
control and suppression of fires occurring on the tracts , nor

thereon .
(3) Subsection ( 2 ) shall not exempt any owner of tim-
berland from assessment by the county under KRS 149.151
to 149.157 .
( 4 ) The degree of protection required on one tract of
land shall not exceed that required on adjoining tracts in the
same protective unit .
Section III. Section 149.120 Kentucky Revised Statutes
is hereby amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) If an owner of timberland , who has elected to pro-
vide a sufficient forest fire patrol , fails to properly patrol it,
the Division of Forestry may have it patrolled and charge the
cost of the patrol against the owner , but the aggregate amount
so charged shall not exceed two cents per acre per year . All
moneys collected from the owners of forest lands , either by
suit or voluntary payment , shall be paid into the State
Treasury .
(2) Subsection ( 1 ) shall not apply to members contribut-
ing to fire protective associations of the state .

County governing bodies may elect to cooperate with the
Division of Forestry , for a minimum period of five years , in
the county -wide control of forest fires , and share in the ex-
pense of establishment and operation of forest fire protection ,
forest fire detection , prevention and suppression . When forest
fire control is established in any county under the terms of
KRS 149.140 to 149.190 , then KRS 149.110 and 149.120 shall
not apply to timberland owners in that county .
Section V. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is hereby created to be known as KRS 149.151 and it
shall read as follows :
Section 149.151 . When a county governing body elects to
cooperate with the Division of Forestry under the terms of
KRS 149.140 its payments for the cost of forest fire protec-
tion , forest fire detection , prevention and suppression re-
quired by KRS 149.190 , may be met out of the general fund
of the county and in addition , the governing body may re-
quire each owner of timberland in the county to participate
directly by paying into a special county forest fire protection
fund an assesment which does not exceed two cents per year
for each acre of timberland of which he is the owner .
Section VI. A new section of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is hereby created to be known as KRS 149.152 and
it shall read as follows :
Section 149.152 . ( 1 ) If the county governing body re-
quires the owners to participate , it shall determine the amount
of the assessment for each acre , and shall order the assess-
ment to be paid into the county treasury by the owners of all
timberland located in the county .
(2 ) It shall be the duty of each owner of timberland
situated in the county to list or have listed such property
with the county tax commissioner between July 1 and Sep-
tember 1 of each year.
(3) Timberland shall be listed by the owner of the first
freehold estate therein, and it shall be listed without reference
to conflicting title . The listing shall be made at the same time
and in the same manner in which declarations of real property

CHAPTER 203 479
CHAPTER 203
(S. B. 235)
AN ACT defining safe burning periods during forest fire seasons .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. It shall be unlawful for any owner or lessee of land
to set fire to , or to procure another to set fire to , any woods ,
brush, logs , leaves , grass , debris , or other inflammable ma-
terial upon such land unless he previously shall have taken
all reasonable care and precaution, by having cut and piled
the same or carefully cleared around the same, to prevent
the spread of such fire to lands other than those owned or
leased by him . It shall also be unlawful for any employee of
any such owner or lessee of land to set fire to or to procure
another to set fire to any woods , brush , logs , leaves , grass ,
debris , or other inflammable material , upon such land unless
he shall have taken similar precautions to prevent the spread
of such fire to any other land .
§ 2. During the months of March , April, May , October ,
November and December , even though the precautions re-
quired by Section One of this Act shall have been taken , it
shall be unlawful , in any county or portion thereof organized
for forest fire control under the direction of the Director of
the Division of Forestry , or designated as a forest fire con-
trol area by such Director , for any person to set fire to , or to
procure another to set fire to , any brush, leaves , grass , debris ,
or field containing dry grass or other inflammable material
capable of spreading fire , located in or within three hundred
feet of any woodland or brush-land , except between the hours
of 4:30 o'clock post meridian and twelve o'clock midnight , or
when the ground is covered with snow .
§ 3. The provisions of Section two of this Act shall not
apply to fires which may be set within the corporate limits of
any town or city in the State , nor to fires set on rights of way
of railroad companies by their duly authorized employees , nor

classification.title: AN ACT defining safe burning periods during forest fire seasons.

Department of Conservation , or his authorized agent , may ,
upon request , and whenever he deems it essential to the best
interests of the people of the Commonwealth , cooperate with
counties , cities , corporations , institutions or individuals in
preparing plans for the protection , management and replace-
ment of trees , woodlands and timber tracts , under an agree-
ment that the parties obtaining such assistance pay at least
the field expenses of the men employed in preparing said
plans . Services rendered under this cooperative plan may in-
clude the designation of trees for sale or removal , measuring
or estimating the commercial volume contained in the trees
designated , marketing advice , and general forestry advice
concerning the management of the land owner's forest .

CHAPTER 205

AN ACT relating to the state parks ; providing for the commissioning of
certain employes of the Division of Parks as peace officers , and pro-
viding a penalty for the violation of regulations governing the parks .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. A new section of the statutes is created to read as
follows :
The Director of the Division of Parks , in his discretion,
may commission the chief custodian of each state park , and
such other of the employes of the division assigned to the
various parks as the director deems necessary , as park patrol-
men , and such custodians and employes , when so commis-
sioned , shall have all the powers of peace officers within the
boundaries of any state park . Any custodian or employe so
commissioned shall be required to execute bond , in a penal
sum fixed by the director , for the faithful and lawful per-
formance of his duties .
§ 2. Section 148.990 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
Any person who violates subsection ( 2 ) of KRS 148.050
shall be fined not less than ten dollars nor more than one
hundred dollars , or imprisoned in jail for not less than one
day nor more than ten days , or both .
Approved March 25 , 1948 .

Voluntary transfer of corporate assets . (1 ) A volun-

(2 ) If the corporation is able to meet its liabilities then

the holders of a majority of the voting power of all share-
holders entitled to vote or by such vote as may be provided

of all shareholders . If the corporation is unable to meet its
liabilities then matured , such authorization may be given by
the vote of the board of directors .
(3) This section is not intended to authorize a convey-
ance or exchange of assets in fraud of corporate creditors or
of minority shareholders or shareholders without voting
rights , or in violation of Chapter 377 , Kentucky Revised
Statutes , relating to bulk sales .

exchange of all of its assets , at a time when it is able to meet
its liabilities then matured , any shareholders who filed with
the corporation written objection thereto before the taking of
the vote on such authorization and whose shares were not
voted in favor of such corporate action may , at any time with-
in twenty days after the date upon which such corporate
action was authorized , demand in writing payment for his
shares .

It also re-enacts KRS 271.500, outlining the process for voluntary corporate dissolution by either the board of directors or by shareholder consent. The amendments to 271.415 and 271.490 apply only to matters authorized by shareholders after the act's effective date.

CHAPTER 207 491
for the purpose of voting on the question of dissolution . At
such a meeting a vote of the shareholders entitled to vote
shall be taken on a motion to dissolve the corporation volun-
tarily , which shall require for its adoption the affirmative vote
of the holders of a majority of the voting power of all share-
holders entitled to vote , or such vote as the articles of incor-
poration may require , which shall not be less than the vote of
the holders of a majority of such voting power . Upon the
adoption of a resolution authorizing dissolution , or upon the
execution of a written consent to dissolution by the holders of
all the voting power of the shareholders , a statement of in-
tent to dissolve shall be signed in triplicate by the president
or a vice -president, and acknowledged by him, and attested by
the secretary or an assistant secretary , which shall set forth ;
(a) The name of the corporation ;
(b ) The names and the post -office addresses of its
officers ;
(c ) The names and the post -office addresses of its
directors ;
(d ) A copy of the resolution of the shareholders auth-
orizing the dissolution, or of the agreement signed by the
shareholders consenting to the dissolution ;
(e) The names and the post -office addresses of the
persons who shall act for the corporation in winding up its
affairs ;
(f) The number of shares outstanding ;
(g) The number of shares voted for and against the
motion for dissolution , respectively , or a statement that the
agreement for dissolution was signed by the holders of all
the voting power of the shareholders in person or by their
attorneys thereunto duly authorized .
Approved March 26 , 1948 .

Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 132.200 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is
amended to read as follows :
All property subject to taxation for state purposes shall
also be subject to taxation in the county , city , school or other
taxing district in which it has a taxable situs , except the
classes of property described in KRS 132.030 and 132.050 ,
and the following classes of property , which shall be subject
to taxation for state purposes only :
(1 ) Farm implements and farm machinery owned by a
person actually engaged in farming and used in his farm
operation .
( 2 ) Livestock and domestic fowls .
(3) Capital stock of building and loan associations .
(4 ) Machinery and products in course of manufacture ,
of individuals or corporations actually engaged in manu-
facturing , and their raw material actually on hand at their
plants for the purpose of manufacture . Individuals or cor-
porations actually engaged in the printing , publication and
distribution of a newspaper or operating a job -printing plant
shall be deemed to be actually engaged in manufacturing .
(5 ) Unmanufactured agricultural products , ( except
that the same shall be exempt from taxation for state pur-
poses to the extent of the value , or amount , of any unpaid
nonrecourse loan or loans thereon granted by the United
States Government or any agency thereof ) , and except that
cities and counties may each impose an ad valorem tax of
not exceeding fifteen cents on each one hundred dollars of
the fair cash value of all unmanufactured agricultural pro-
ducts subject to taxation within their limits that are not
actually on hand at the plants of manufacturing concerns
for the purpose of manufacture , nor in the hands of the pro-
ducer or any agent of the producer to whom the products
have been conveyed or assigned for the purpose of sale .
(6) Money in hand , notes , bonds , accounts and other
credits , whether secured by mortgage , pledge or otherwise ,
or unsecured , and shares of stock , except that nothing in this

CHAPTER 208
(S. B. 242 )
AN ACT relating to licensing of motor vehicle operators .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 186.410 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) Every person except those exempted by KRS 186.-
420 , 186.430 and 186.450 , before operating a motor vehicle
upon a highway, shall secure an operator's license at the
time and for the periods specified as follows :
(a) Persons whose family names begin with the letters
"A" to " K" inclusive shall before August 1 , 1952 , secure
a license for the ensuing two year period , and biennially
thereafter . The fee for this license shall be $ 2.00 .
(b ) Persons whose family names begin with the letters
"L" through " Z" inclusive shall before August 1 , 1952 , se-
cure a license for one year for which the fee shall be $1.00 .
Such persons shall before August 1 , 1953 secure a license for
the ensuing two year period and biennially thereafter . The
fee for this license shall be $2.00 .
( c ) Any person whose family name shall be changed
from one alphabetical group to the other hereinabove de-
scribed shall have his license renewed for such period as will
place him in the proper alphabetical group . The fee for this
renewal shall be $ 1.00 .
(d ) The fee for original licenses obtained after August
1 , 1952 , may be pro- rated in accordance with regulations
which the Department shall prescribe .
entries.citation: Acts of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky, 1948, p. 508

The act also outlines procedures for license renewals when a family name changes and for pro-rating fees for original licenses obtained after a certain date.
classification.reasoning: The statute clearly outlines a system for motor vehicle operator licensing based on the alphabetical order of family names. There is no mention of race, nor are there any provisions that would implicitly or explicitly discriminate based on race. The differentiation in licensing periods and fees is solely based on the initial letter of a person's last name. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law.

(1 ) The Highway Patrol may examine every applicant
for an operator's license except as otherwise provided in this

the applicant resides unless the applicant is granted written
permission by the circuit clerk of the county in which he re-

CHAPTER 209
(S. B. 243)
AN ACT relating to judicial districts ; creating an additional district
and making changes in the apportionment of counties to the Twenty-
sixth , Thirty -third and Thirty -fifth Districts in order to comply
with the meaning and intent of the Constitution with respect to
division of the state into judicial districts ; providing for the con-
tinuation in office of the present circuit judges and Commonwealth's
attorneys and creating the offices of additional circuit judge and
Commonwealth's attorney for the new district established by this
Act ; and providing for the terms of court in each district affected
by this Act.
WHEREAS , the General Assembly has given due regard to the ele-
ments of territory , business and population involved in the di-
vision of the state into judicial districts ; and
WHEREAS , with such due regard , the General Assembly has found
the following facts to exist :
1. That the entire population of the state , as of the last preceding
Federal Census , is 2,845,627 .
2. That upon the basis of such population , additional judicial dis-
tricts may be established to those now existing , pursuant to
Sections 128 and 132 of the Constitution .
entries.citation: Acts of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky, 1948, p. 511

classification.title: AN ACT relating to judicial districts
classification.summary: This act creates an additional judicial district and reallocates counties among the Twenty-sixth, Thirty-third, and Thirty-fifth Judicial Districts to comply with constitutional requirements regarding the division of the state into judicial districts. It also addresses the continuation of current judges and attorneys and the creation of new positions for the new district, along with specifying terms of court. Additionally, it amends KRS 186.530 by repealing it and amends KRS 186.600 to prohibit individuals under twenty-one from driving school buses or public passenger vehicles.
classification.reasoning: The provided text contains two distinct sections. The first section (Sections 8-10) amends Kentucky Revised Statutes related to driver's licenses and the operation of school buses and public passenger vehicles, setting an age restriction of twenty-one for operating such vehicles. The second section (Chapter 209) deals with the restructuring of judicial districts. Neither section contains any explicit or implicit references to race or racial segregation. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law.

CHAPTER 209 497
3. That , as of the last Federal Census , the population of Harlan
County is 75,275 and the population of Pike County is 71,122 ;
that both of such counties are industrial counties in which there
is an abnormal amount of litigation ; and that the volume of
business in the courts of such counties is such that , when com-
bined with the business of any other county , it is impossible for
the courts to transact the business without a long period of delay
and serious prejudice to the rights of litigants who are unable
to obtain reasonably prompt disposition of their litigation .
4. That the territory embracing the counties of Bell and Leslie
is such as to make it convenient and advantageous that those
counties be joined in one judicial district ; the population of those
counties is such as is contemplated by the Constitution to be
included in one judicial district ; and the business of the courts in
those counties will be expedited by combining those counties
in one judicial district .
5 That the territory embracing the counties of Letcher and Perry
is such as to make it convenient and advantageous that those
counties be joined in one judicial district ; the population of
those counties is such as is contemplated by the Constitution
to be included in one judicial district ; and the business of the
courts in those counties will be expedited by combining those
counties in one judicial district ; and
WHEREAS , the General Assembly finds it to be the intent and purpose
of Sections 128 and 132 of the Constitution that the General As-
sembly may establish such number of judicial districts as may be
required by reason of the exigencies of territory , business and
population , not exceeding one district for each 60,000 of popula-
tion of the state , excluding the districts in counties having a popu-
lation of 150,000 ; and that the Constitution contemplates that wheth-
er there shall be more than one county in any judicial district de-
pends solely upon the exigencies of territory , business and popula-
tion , with the express intention that the population in any dis-
trict be as near to 60,000 as is practicable , regardless of the num-
ber of counties therein , and with the exception prescribed in
Section 138 of the Constitution that certain counties shall have the
privilege of constituting separate districts even though their popula-
tion is not as much as 60,000 ;
NOW THEREFORE
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. The twenty - sixth , thirty -third and thirty -fifth judi-
cial Districts shall hereafter be composed of the following
counties :
Twenty -sixth District . Harlan
Thirty -third District . Letcher and Perry
Thirty -fifth District . Pike

CHAPTER 211
(S. B. 247 )
AN ACT relating to the Sheriff's county revenue bond , providing for
payment of premium on such bond by the county where the Sheriff
executes a bond with corporate surety .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Each sheriff who is required by law to furnish at
his own expense a bond for the collection of county revenue
shall have a claim against the county for the amount paid for
the premium on his county revenue bond when the bond has
been executed by an incorporated surety company authorized
to do a surety business in Kentucky , and when the claim is
verified by his affidavit .
Approved March 25 , 1948 .

CHAPTER 213
(S. B. 255 )
AN ACT relating to police court judge and police court judge pro tem
in cities of the first class .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 26.220 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is hereby repealed and re -enacted and as so re-
enacted shall read :
The judge of the Police Court of a city of the first class
may appoint a pro tem judge of the police court . The pro
tem judge shall hold office at the pleasure of the judge , and
shall have the same rights , powers and duties as the regular
judge , whenever the regular judge fails to attend and hold
court , or vacates the bench . The pro tem judge shall not
practice any case in the police court . The pro tem judge
shall have the same qualifications as the regular judge , and
shall receive a salary of three thousand dollars per annum ,
payable semi -monthly by the city .
Section 2. This Act shall not take effect until December
1 , 1949 .
Approved March 25 , 1948 .

CHAPTER 214 505
Section 1. Sections 69.430 and 69.440 of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes are hereby repealed and re -enacted and
as so re -enacted shall read :
The prosecuting attorney of the police court in cities
of the first class shall be elected by the qualified voters of
the city at the same time and places at which the Mayor is
elected , beginning in the year 1949 at the general elections
of said year, and shall hold office for the term of four years .
In all matters coming before the police court the prosecut-
ing attorney shall represent the commonwealth or the city,
as the case may require , but the Commonwealth's attorney
or the county attorney may be present and assist in the trial
of offenses against the Commonwealth . The prosecuting at-
torney shall have the qualifications , take the oath and come
under the obligations prescribed for Commonwealth's at-
torneys .
When said prosecuting attorney fails to attend or to
prosecute the parties brought before him, the judge shall ,
from the members of the bar , appoint one to serve as pro
tem prosecuting attorney , and the person so appointed shall
take the oath of office prescribed for the regular prosecut-
ing attorney and for his services the pro tem prosecuting at-
torney shall receive such part of the salary of the regular
prosecuting attorney as the judge, on motion , after due notice
to the prosecuting attorney may summarily decide to be fair
and reasonable ; but the compensation allowed the pro tem
prosecuting attorney shall not be estimated at a greater rate
than that allowed the regular prosecuting attorney . The
amount so paid the pro tem prosecuting attorney shall be
deducted from the salary of the regular prosecuting attor-
ney : Provided that during the months July and August of
each year the prosecuting attorney may have vacation and
during said vacation the compensation of the pro tem pros-
ecuting attorney shall not be deducted from the salary of the
regular prosecuting attorney .
A salary of Five Thousand ( $ 5000.00 ) Dollars per an-
num shall be paid the prosecuting attorney and he shall be
paid at the same time and in the same manner as the salaries
of other city officers are paid .

chapter , unless the context clearly requires otherwise and
subject to the other provisions of this section , " covered em-
ployment " means service , including service in interstate com-
merce , performed for wages or under any contract of hire ,
written or oral , express or implied in which the relationship
of the individual performing such service and the employing
unit for which such services are rendered is , as to those serv-
ices , the legal relationship of employer and employe .
(2) " Covered employment " includes a worker's entire
service performed within or both within and without this
state if :
(a) The service is localized in this state ; or
(b) The service is not localized in any state , but some
of the service is performed in this state , and :
1. The base of operations , or , if there is no base of
operations , then the place from which such service is directed
or controlled , is in this state ; or
2. The base of operations or place from which such
service is directed or controlled is not in any state in which
some part of the service is performed , but the worker's resi-
dence is in this state .
( 3 ) Services not covered under subsections ( 1 ) or ( 2 )
of this section , and performed entirely without this state ,
with respect to no part of which contributions are required
and paid under an unemployment compensation law of any
other state or of the Federal Government , are covered em-
ployment if the worker performing them is a resident of this
state and if the commission approves the election of the em-

SUBJECT EMPLOYER . As used in this chapter , un-
less the context clearly requires otherwise , " subject employ-
er" means :
(1) Any employing unit which, in each of three calen-
dar quarters in the preceding calendar year , had in covered
employment four or more workers (whether or not the same
workers ) , to as many as four of whom at least fifty dollars
in wages was payable to each with respect to such covered
employment in each such quarter .
(2 ) Any employing unit which for some portion of a
day (but not necessarily simultaneously ) in each of twenty
different weeks ( whether or not such weeks are consecutive )
within either the current or the preceding calendar year , has
or had in covered employment eight or more workers ( wheth-
er the same workers are or were employed in each such day ) .
( 3 ) Any employing unit that succeeds to the organiza-
tion , trade or business of another employing unit which at
the time of such succession is a subject employer ; and any
employing unit which succeeds to the organization , trade , or
business of any previously separate portion of another em-
ploying unit , which portion , if treated as a separate employ-
ing unit , would be , at the time of the succession , a subject
employer under subsections ( 1 ) or ( 2 ) of this section .
(4 ) Any employing unit that succeeds to any of the
assets of a subject employer's business by any means what-
ever , otherwise than in the ordinary course of trade , is a sub-
ject employer if the commission finds that :
(a ) The transferee has continued or resumed the bus-
iness of the transferor , either in the same establishment or
elsewhere ; and
(b) The transferee has employed substantially the same
workers as those the transferor had employed in connection
with the assets acquired ; and
(c ) The transferee has acquired a previously separate
portion of the transferor's assets , which portion , if treated
as a separate employing unit , would be , at the time of the
succession , a subject employer under subsections ( 1 ) or ( 2 )
of this section .

Section 3. That section 341.090 of the Kentucky Re-
vised Statutes be repealed and re -enacted , so that when re-
enacted the same shall read as follows :
BASE PERIOD , BENEFIT YEAR AND BASE-
PERIOD WAGES . As used in this chapter , unless the con-
text clearly requires otherwise :
( 1 ) "Base period " means the first four of the last five
completed calendar quarters immediately preceding the first
day of a worker's benefit year .
(2) " Benefit year . " " Benefit year " for any worker
means the period of time beginning with the date as of which
he first files a valid claim for benefits and ending one full year
later , and thereafter, the period of time beginning with the
date as of which he next files a valid claim for benefits after
the termination of the last preceding benefit year and ending
one full year later , except that any claim filed as of February
29 of any year shall be construed to have been filed as of Feb-
ruary 28 of such year . As used in this subsection " valid
claim " means any initial claim for benefits if the worker has
satisfied the conditions required under subsection ( 5 ) of KRS
341.350 .
(3) " Base -period wages " means the wages paid to a
worker during his base period by subject employers for
covered employment . The commission may , with respect to
this subsection , consider wages paid to mean wages payable
in order to prevent inequities in particular cases .

formed by individuals in its employ in one or more distinct

stitute covered employment by a subject employer for all the

shall cease to be considered covered employment subject here-

Section 12. That section 341.370 of the Kentucky Re-

CONDITIONS OF BENEFIT DISQUALIFICATION .
( 1 ) A worker shall be disqualified from receiving benefits or

(b ) He has left his most recent suitable work volun-

( c ) He has been discharged for misconduct connected

or prior to the expiration of the period for appeal , except
that , if a referee affirms a determination allowing benefits ,
they shall be paid until or unless the decision of the referee
is reversed by the commission . If benefits are paid pursuant
to a decision of the referee that is finally reversed in any
subsequent proceedings with respect thereto , no employer's
account shall be charged wilth benefits so paid but such bene-
fits shall be charged against the pooled account .
(4 ) Unless such appeal is withdrawn , a referee , after
affording the parties reasonable opportunity for a fair hear-
ing, shall affirm or modify the determination . The parties
shall be duly notified of his decision , together with the reasons
therefor , which shall be deemed to be the final decision of the
commission , unless within ten days after the date of mailing
of such decision , further appeal is initiated under KRS
341.430 .

remedies before the commission , secure judicial review there-
of by filing a petition for review against the commission in
the circuit court of the county in which the claimant was last
employed by a subject employer whose reserve account is
affected by such claims . Any other party to the proceedings
before the commission shall be made a defendant in such
action . The petition shall state fully the grounds upon which
review is sought , assign all errors relied on , and shall be veri-
fied by the petitioner . The petitioner shall furnish copies
thereof for each defendant to the commission , which shall
deliver one copy to each defendant .
(2) Summons shall issue upon the petition directing the
commission to file answer within fifteen days after service

mission shall maintain within the fund a separate reserve
account for each subject employer , and shall , except as pro-

of all contributions paid by the employer on his own behalf .
The liability of each such account shall be limited to the pay-
ment of benefits to eligible workers .
(2 ) Except as provided in subsection ( 3 ) of this section
the benefits paid to an ligible worker for a week of unemploy-
ment shall be charged proportionately against the accounts
of those of his subject employers from whom he has base-
period wages . The proportion so used shall be that ratio
which is obtained by dividing the base-period wages of a
worker from such employer by the total base -period wages
of such worker , adjusted to the nearest multiple of one per-
cent . For the purpose of this subsection a fractional part of
a cent shall be disregarded unless it amounts to one -half cent
or more , in which case it shall be increased to one cent .
(3) Notwithstanding the provisions of subsection ( 2 ) of
this section , benefits paid to an eligible worker and chargeable
to an employer's reserve account under such subsection shall

Section 24. That section 341.660 of the Kentucky Re-
vised Statutes is amended to read as follows :
PLEDGE OF FUNDS ACCUMULATING UNDER
KRS 341.290 , AND RENTS ; USE OF SURPLUS . (1 ) All
funds which accumulate under KRS 341.290 from and after
July 1 , 1946 are hereby set aside and pledged as a separate
and special fund to be used and applied in the payment of
interest on and principal of the bonds provided for in KRS
341.630 and KRS 341.640 . In addition thereto , upon the occu-
pancy by the commission of any building constructed with
the proceeds of the bonds herein referred to , the commission
shall, by resolution , set aside and pledge in a separate account
in the Unemployment Compensation Administration fund all
monies received thenceforth from any agencies of the Federal
Government which are designated to be paid as rental for the
occupancy of such building . Such resolution shall also pledge
that the commission will properly maintain and operate said
building and carry sufficient insurance thereon to secure the
payment of said bonds in the event said building should be
damaged or destroyed . Provided , however , that all or any
portion of the separate and special fund until encumbered by
the issuance of the bonds provided for in KRS 341.630 and
KRS 341.640 or any portion of such fund which may be
released by any means whatsoever from any encumbrance
heretofore made may , by order of the commission , be used or
pledged for the purpose of carrying out the provisions of
KRS 341.630 or may be used for administrative purposes .
( 2 ) After such bonds are issued , in the event any sur-
plus shall be accumulated in said special fund in excess of the
amount of interest and principal payments coming due in the
next calendar year , any such surplus shall be used to pay off
and retire outstanding bonds .
Section 25. That paragraph (b ) of the item headed
DEPARTMENT OF INDUSTRIAL RELATIONS of PART
TWO of House Bill No. 5 of the 1948 General Assembly is
repealed .

CHAPTER 217
(S. B. 285)
AN ACT prescribing the time within which appeals shall be taken to
the Court of Appeals from judgments and appealable orders of cir-
cuit courts involving questions arising under the planning and zoning
laws of this Commonwealth .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Appeals to the Court of Appeals from judg-
ments and appealable orders of the circuit courts arising
under the planning and zoning laws of this Commonwealth
shall be taken in the same manner and within the same time
provided for appeals from declaratory judgments by Section
639a -5 of the Civil Code of Practice , and all provisions of said
Section of the Civil Code of Practice shall apply to such
appeals .
Section 2. This Act shall apply to such judgments and
appealable orders heretofore rendered or which may here-
after be rendered ; provided , however , that as to judgments
and appealable orders entered prior to the effective date of
this Act, the period of sixty days shall begin to run from the
effective date hereof.

upon , marring , handling or carrying away of any rock , wall , or
other formation within any cave exhibited to the public as a natural
attraction , without the consent of the owner .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Any person who , without permission from the owner ,
shall destroy , break, tear down , write upon , mar, handle or
carry away any rock, wall , or other formation within any
cave exhibited to the public as a natural attraction shall be
fined not less than five dollars nor more than one hundred
dollars , or imprisoned not less than five nor more than thirty
days , or both .
Approved March 26 , 1948 .

in so far as it relates to aeronautics ; and repealing KRS 183.020 ,
183.030 , 183.031 , 183.033 , and 183.040 .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. There hereby is created and established the
Department of Aeronautics , which shall constitute a statutory
administrative department of the State Government within
the meaning of KRS , Chapter 12 , and which department shall
exercise all of the administrative functions of the Common-
wealth in relation to aeronautics , including aircraft , air trans-
portation , airports , air navigation , and all matters related to
said functions . The Department of Aeronautics shall be head-
ed by the Commissioner of Aeronautics , who shall be the
executive officer and have sole charge of the administration
of the Department and the performance of all functions of
the Department. No person shall be appointed as Commis-
sioner unless at the time of his appointment he holds a valid
commercial license , or its equivalent , issued by the United
States Government for the piloting of aircraft , and unless
he shall have had at least five (5) years ' experience in aero-
nautics .
Section 2. The Commissioner of Aeronautics , with the
advice and approval of the Governor , may appoint an Ad-
visory Board of Aeronautics to advise with the Commissioner
in carrying out the functions of the Department . Such Board ,
if appointed , shall consist of not less than three ( 3 ) nor more
than nine ( 9 ) members , who shall serve for such terms , not
exceeding four ( 4 ) years , as may be fixed by the Commis-
sioner , and who shall be removable at the will of the Com-
missioner . The Commissioner shall serve as Chairman of the
Board , and the Board shall meet at the call of the Chairman .

CHAPTER 226
(S. B. 311 )
AN ACT to amend Sections 58.020 and 58.030 KRS , which sections are
part of an Act providing for the acquisition and development of
public projects by governmental units and agencies through revenue
bonds , by inserting the words " or administration " between the
words " legislative " and "body " in the third sentence of said section
KRS 58.020 , and by changing the word "twenty -five " to "forty" in
KRS 58.030 ( 1 ) .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
That Section 58.020 KRS be amended so that when
amended it shall read as follows :
A governmental agency acting separately or jointly with
one or more of any such agency , may acquire , construct , main-
tain, add to and improve any public project as defined in
KRS 58.010 , which public project may be located within or
without or partly within and partly without the territorial
limits of such governmental agency or agencies and for the
purpose of defraying the cost thereof may borrow money
and issue negotiable revenue bonds . Before any department
or agency of the state government shall borrow money and
issue bonds under KRS 58.010 to 58.140 , the head of the
department or agency shall file with the Secretary of State
a written order , approved by the Governor , the Attorney-
General and the Commissioner of Finance , setting forth the
proposed public project , the amount of the bonds to be issued ,
and the maximum rate of interest the bonds are to bear . Any
governmental agency other than agencies of the state govern-
ment may borrow money and issue revenue bonds under KRS
58.010 to 58.140 pursuant to an order , resolution or ordinance
of its legislative or administrative body , which order , resolu-
tion or ordinance shall set forth the proposed public project ,
the amount of the revenue bonds to be issued and the maxi-
mum rate of interest . In every instance the order , resolution
or ordinance shall provide that the public project is being
undertaken under the provisions of KRS 58.010 to 58.140 .
(1946 , c . 126 section 2 ) .

Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
That Section 148.020 KRS be amended so that when
amended it shall read as follows :
The Division of Parks may :
(1 ) acquire lands by purchase , lease or otherwise , on
which to operate state parks ;
(2 ) improve said parks by constructing and equipping
improvements and facilities in said parks ;
( 3 ) operate said parks , and fix fees and charges for
the use of said parks and the improvements and facilities
constructed therein , and provide for the collection of said
fees and charges and the proper accounting therefor .
That section 148.030 KRS be amended so that when
amended it shall read as follows :
(1 ) The cost of acquiring such parks and constructing
improvements and facilities therein and equipping same may
be defrayed by funds received from appropriations , gifts ,
the use of money received as fees and charges for the use of
said parks and facilities , or by the issuance of revenue bonds ,
or by a combination of such sources of funds .
( 2 ) In the event revenue bonds are issued to defray
all or any part of such costs , said revenue bonds shall be
issued pursuant to the terms of KRS 58.010 to 58.140 .
( 3 ) The Division of Parks may unite into one project
for financing purposes all or as many parks , and the improve-
ments thereon , or to be constructed , enlarged or improved , as
it deems practicable , so that the fees and charges and other
revenue or receipts from every source whatsoever from the
parks thus united shall be used for the payment of the prin-
cipal and interest of all bonds which may be issued . Such
united receipts shall continue until all bonds issued have been
paid in full . The lien of the bonds for such united project
shall be a lien upon the gross income and revenue of all the
parks thus united into a project .
(4 ) All parks in which improvements and facilities are
constructed and equipped by the issuance of revenue bonds

AN ACT for the consolidation of the offices of sheriff and jailer in
counties containing a city of the first class .

wealth of Kentucky :

taining a city of the first class shall be consolidated , but the

CHAPTER 233 559
Upon the consolidation of the offices of sheriff and jailer
and ' the establishment of a workhouse or penal farm in any
county containing a city of the first class , no person there-
after , except in cases of contempt of court, shall be sentenced
to the county jail , nor shall serve any time therein . The jail
in such county shall function only as a holdover or station
house for the retention of persons awaiting trial , sentence
or transfer , and shall be administered by the sheriff by his
appointment of a superintendent therefor experienced in
penal administration . The sheriff shall receive the same fees
as jailers receive for the care and maintenance of said per-
sons so confined and said fees and the other fees of his office
shall be available for the operation thereof . It shall be the
duty of the fiscal court of such county to appropriate money
from the general funds for the operation of such jail or
holdover in event the fees of his office are insufficient properly
to care for the persons confined therein . Any city within the
county may use such jail upon payment of the fees set out in
KRS 64.150 .
Approved March 25 , 1948 .
CHAPTER 233
(S. B. 321 )
AN ACT providing for the acquisition , use , operation and administration
of a workhouse or penal farm in counties containing a city of the
first class .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
1. The fiscal court of any county containing a city
of the first class may purchase sufficient land any place within
said county for use as a workhouse or penal farm and have
constructed thereon facilities for the housing and rehabilita-
tion of persons convicted by any of the courts in such county .
The cost of such land and facilities shall be paid out of the
county levy. A superintendent experienced in penal adminis-
tration appointed by the county director of welfare and re-
movable only for good cause shall supervise said institution ,
and employ the necessary personnel for the operation thereof .

It mandates that after such a facility is established, individuals will no longer be sentenced to the county jail, except for contempt of court. The jail will then serve as a temporary holding facility. The sheriff will administer this holdover and receive fees for the care of confined individuals, with the fiscal court responsible for supplementing funds if fees are insufficient. Cities within the county can also use the jail upon payment of fees.

CHAPTER 234
(S. B. 322)
AN ACT providing for the parole of persons confined in the workhouse
or penal farm for counties containing a city of the first class .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
The director of welfare of any county containing a city
of the first class may promulgate rules and regulations for
the creation , supervision and operation of a system of parole
whereby there may be deduction for good conduct from the
time of persons confined in the workhouse or penal farm of
such county by reason of a judgment of any court within such
county , notwithstanding KRS 439.090 . Provided however , said
system shall not become effective until such rules and regula-
tions are approved by the fiscal court of such counties and
may be amended by a similar procedure . If there is no di-
rector of welfare in such county , the fiscal court shall have
the exclusive authority to promulgate such rules and regu-
lations .
Approved March 25 , 1948 .

CHAPTER 235
(S. B. 332 )
AN ACT relating to budget and financial administration of the State
Government , with particular relation to revolving , trust and agency
funds ; amending KRS 45.140 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 45.140 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is
amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) Revolving fund accounts may be established by
appropriations for financing activities self-supporting in
whole or in part . The operating receipts of such activities
shall be deposited in the State Treasury to the credit of the
revolving fund so established .
( 2) Trust and agency fund accounts may be established
to receive and disburse contributions , gifts , donations , devises
and Federal appropriations , and , when authorized by law ,
by depositing all of the fees (which include fees for main-
tcnance in state institutions , incidental fees , tuition fees , fees
for board and room , athletics , and student activities ) , rentals ,
admittance , sales , licenses collected by law, subventions and
other miscellaneous receipts of such boards , commissions ,
departments , agencies and institutions .
( 3 ) The head of the budget unit or other responsible
fiscal agent of the unit or division for which a revolving , trust
or agency fund has been established shall deposit with the
State Treasury all receipts of the character above described ,
and the Department of Finance and State Treasury shall
credit all such receipts to the budget unit or division thereof
as directed in writing by the head of the budget unit or divi-
sion thereof, and shall keep a separate account for each fund
so established .
(4 ) The amounts credited to any revolving , trust or
agency fund so provided , together with any amount appro-
priated therefor , may be expended for the purposes of the
fund without further specific appropriation . All money re-
ceived for the fund , except direct appropriations from the
state , shall at all times be reserved in the State Treasury

CHAPTER 236
(S. B. 340)
AN ACT relating to the availability of state funds appropriated for
public assistance , including old age assistance , aid to dependent
children , and aid to the needy blind .
WHEREAS , social and economic factors make it exceedingly difficult to
estimate exactly the number of persons who may be determined
eligible to receive assistance in a particular year , the full amount
of assistance funds appropriated by the Legislature and intended for
needy persons may not be distributed to them , unless provision is
made to avoid these monies being turned back to the General Fund
at the end of each year with consequent loss of matching federal
funds .
NOW THEREFORE ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
SECTION 1 : A new section of the statutes regarding
aid to dependent children is created , to be numbered KRS
200.045 and to read as follows :
Notwithstanding the provisions of KRS 45.230 and not-
withstanding any other provision of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes , any unexpended or unencumbered balance of any
Social Security appropriation , made available for expend-
iture by the Division of Public Assistance for the first fiscal
year of each biennium, remaining at the end of such fiscal
year , shall be carried forward and be available for expend-
iture at any time during the ensuing fiscal year within the
biennium and no portion shall lapse to the General Expend-
iture Fund .

564 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
1. There is hereby created an independent Agency of
the Commonwealth of Kentucky under Chapter 12 of the
Kentucky Revised Statutes , which shall be known as the Ken-
tucky Building Commission and which shall be composed of
the Governor , who shall be Chairman thereof , the Lieutenant
Governor , the Attorney General, the Commissioner of Fi-
nance and the Commissioner of Revenue .
1
2. Such Commission is authorized , empowered and di-
rected to determine the comparative need and demand for
acquiring lands , the construction or reconstruction of build-
ings , the installation of utility services , including roads and
sewers , the purchase and installation of equipment , facilities
and furnishings for the buildings so constructed or recon-
structed , or for existing buildings , which equipment , facilities
and furnishings are to be of a permanent nature . After the
Kentucky Building Commission has determined the need and
demand for any one or all of the purposes herein set forth
for any agency or institution hereinafter referred to , it is
authorized and empowered to provide for and adopt such
plans and specifications as may be necessary , to advertise
for and receive bids for any expenditures proposed to be
made , to award contracts for the purpose herein authorized ,
to supervise construction and make such changes or revisions
in plans and specifications , or in construction, as may become
necessary , and generally to do any and all other things as
may become necessary or expedient in order to effectively
fulfill and carry out the purpose for which it is created ,
including the right to employ such clerks , engineers , statis-
ticians , architects or other persons required to be employed
in order to fulfill the objective of its creation and further to
adopt such rules and regulations as may be expedient and
which are not in conflict with its general purposes . The ad-
vertisement for bids and the receipt thereof and the awarding
of contracts thereon shall be in accordance with Chapters 12 ,
42 and 45 of the KRS . Warrants for such expenditures shall
be issued by the Commissioner of Finance on the State
Treasury upon an order of the Commission .

any department , institution or agency thereof , and the Com-

(6) The Commission may lease any real estate or any
building , or any part of a building , now owned or hereafter
acquired by the Commonwealth to any person , firm or cor-
poration , not a department , institution or agency of the State ,
when it finds as a matter of fact that neither said real estate
nor any building or part thereof is needed for State purposes .
Any such lease or rental agreement shall be upon such terms
and conditions and for such consideration as the Commission
may prescribe and the same shall be made in the name of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , and shall be signed by the Chair-
man of the Commission and lodged for record in the office
of the Secretary of State .
(7) The Commission is authorized and empowered to
issue and sell revenue bonds in such denominations and in
such amount or amounts as may be deemed by the Commis-
sion to be for the best interest of the Commonwealth for any
of the following purposes :
(a) To acquire real estate .
(b) To construct any building or other improvement
authorized by Section 2 of this Act .
(c) To pay all or any part of the expenses or cost inci-
dental to such purchase , building or construction, and
(d) To defray the cost of plans , specifications , blue-
prints , architectural fees and other expenses authorized to
be incurred under the terms of this Act.
Any revenue bonds so issued shall bear interest at a rate
not exceeding 5 per cent per annum , payable semi -annually,
and shall become due and payable not more than forty years
from the date of issuance . The payment of said bonds , to-
gether with the interest thereon , may be secured by a pledge
and a first lien on all of the receipts and revenues derived , or
to be derived , from the rental or operation of said properties .

574 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Whan a Board of Education of an independent
school district desires to have its district become a part of
the county school district , it shall by motion so record its
desire in the Minutes of the Board . The Board , or its execu-
tive officer , shall convey this request to the County Board
of Education . At its next regular meeting , or at a special
meeting held prior thereto , the County Board of Education
shall pass upon this request .
§ 2. If the County Board of Education refuses , or the
two Boards of Education cannot agree upon such a proposi-
tion of merger of the independent district with the county
district , the independent Board of Education may appeal to
the Superintendent of Public Instruction and ask that its
request to become a part of the County School District be
submitted to the State Board of Education for final settle-
ment .
§ 3. If the State Board of Education approves the
proposition of merger , it shall become effective and the inde-
pendent district shall become a part of the county school
district on the basis of the conditions of merger as set out
by action of the State Board of Education .
Approved March 25 , 1948 .

Section XI. The Department may make such rules and
regulations as are necessary and proper to carry out and
foster the provisions of this Act.
Section XII . The provisions of Section 56.030 of the
Kentucky Revised Statutes do not apply to land or interest
in land or easements thereon partly or wholly paid for out
of state funds used in the construction , alteration , or develop-
ment of airports in the State Airport Plan .
Section XIII . There is hereby created in the State
Treasury a special fund to be known as the KENTUCKY
AIRPORT DEVELOPMENT FUND . All moneys deposited
or paid into this fund , including repayment of any loans
made therefrom , are appropriated and shall be available to
the Department for the purposes of this Act . All moneys in
such fund shall be expended solely for the development of
airports in the State Airport Plan . The fund shall consist of
all money appropriated by the State and all money received
from the United States or from any other source for the
purpose of airport development . All moneys in the Kentucky
Airport Development Fund shall be deposited , administered
and disbursed in the same manner and under the same con-
ditions and requirements as are provided by law for other
special funds in the State Treasury , except that any balance
in this fund shall not lapse at any time but shall be con-
tinuously available to the Department for the purposes of
carrying out the provisions of this Act. A general statement
that all continuing appropriations are repealed shall not be
construed as abolishing this fund .
Section XIV . There is hereby appropriated out of the
surplus cash balance which will be in the State Treasury to
the credit of the General Expenditure Fund , as of June 30 ,
1948 , the sum of two -hundred and fifty thousand dollars
($250,000.00 ) for the fiscal year beginning July 1 , 1948 , and
ending June 30 , 1949 , the sum of two -hundred and fifty thou-
sand dollars ( $ 250,000.00 ) for the fiscal year beginning July
1 , 1949 , and ending June 30 , 1950 , said sums being appro-

A CONCURRENT RESOLUTION providing for the purchase and dis-
tribution of "The Legislative Digest ."
Be it Resolved by the House of Representatives of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , the Senate Concurring Therein ,
That :
§ 1. The Perry Publishing Company is authorized and
directed to furnish 200 copies of " The Legislative Digest " ,
each day the House or Senate is in session , for delivery and
distribution as provided in Section 2 of this Resolution , for
which said company shall be paid the sum of $3,500 out of
the appropriation for expense of legislative sessions for the

The Department of Finance shall issue its warrant for pay-
ment of this amount upon receipt of a voucher for the same
endorsed and approved by the Speaker of the House of
Representatives and the President of the Senate .
§ 2. The distribution of " The Legislative Digest " shall
be as follows :
(a) 110 copies to the Clerk of the House for distribu-
tion to the members of the House and to such officers of the
House as the Speaker may direct .
(b) 45 copies to the Clerk of the Senate for distribution
to the members of the Senate and to such officers of the
Senate as the President of the Senate may direct .
(c ) 45 copies to the Governor and to such officers and
employes of the Commonwealth as the Governor , the Pres-
ident of the Senate and the Speaker of the House may direct .
Approved January 22 , 1948 .

CHAPTER 243
(H. R. 6 )
A JOINT RESOLUTION providing for the rental or lease of a loud-
speaker system for the use of the House of Representatives during
the 1948 Regular Session , and authorizing the expenditure of a por-
tion of the Legislative Expense appropriation for that purpose .
WHEREAS , a loudspeaker system is essential for the efficient and
expeditious conduct of the business of the House of Representatives :
NOW THEREFORE
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
That the Speaker and the Clerk of the House of Repre-
sentatives are authorized to contract for the rental or lease
of a loudspeaker system , at a cost not to exceed $500 , for
the use of the House of Representatives during the 1948
Regular Session .
That the cost of such rental or lease , not to exceed the
sum above specified , shall be paid out of the appropriation
for legislative expense for the 1948 Regular Session , and the
Department of Finance , upon receipt of a voucher for the
same, approved by the Speaker and the Clerk of the House ,
shall issue its warrant upon the State Treasurer , against
said appropriation , payable to the person from whom the
system is so rented or leased .
WHEREAS a loudspeaker system is urgently needed
for the conduct of the business of the House , which is now
in session , an emergency is declared to exist and this Resolu-
tion shall take effect upon its passage and approval by the
Governor .
Not approved .
Received by Secretary of State February 21 , 1948 .

CHAPTER 251 589
That Charles V. Hall while duly elected and acting as
Sheriff of Gallatin County, Kentucky , did on the 26th day of
November 1944 , as provided by Law and instructed by the
proper Court , transport the bodies of three prisoners ,
to -wit : Hezzie Ervin , Ze Martin and Huston Duff , from the
said Gallatin County, Kentucky , to the Kentucky State Re-
formatory at LaGrange , Ky ., for which services he would in
accordance with the terms of KRS 64.070 be entitled to a
reimbursement from the State Treasurer in the amount of
$ 21.50 ; that on the 29th day of June 1945 the said Hall while
acting in the same capacity and same direction did transport
the body of one Elmer Scharfenberger from the said Gallatin
County, Kentucky , to the Kentucky State Reformatory at
LaGrange , Kentucky , and that under said provision of Law
the said Hall would be entitled to a reimbursement of $ 11.25
from the Treasurer of the State of Kentucky .
It appearing that said claims for reimbursement were not
presented for payment by the Commonwealth of Kentucky
within the statutory time as provided by Law, and it further
appearing the said claims are just and honest claims against
the Commonwealth of Kentucky , the Commissioner of Finance
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky is hereby authorized and
directed to draw his warrant upon the State Treasurer in the
sum of $32.75 in favor of the said Charles V. Hall in payment
of the said claims and no provision , or provisions , of Law or
regulation shall bar the said payment and the amount neces-
sary to pay said claims , being $32.75 , is hereby appropriated
out of the General Fund for that purpose .
Approved March 26, 1948 .

594 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
That Richmond Salyer , of Salyersville , Kentucky , is
authorized to sue the Commonwealth of Kentucky in the
Magoffin Circuit Court for personal injuries and damage to
automobile , alleged to have been caused by the Department
of Highways and its employees in that a State Highway Truck
ran into his automobile , thereby injuring said Richmond
Salyer and damaging his automobile .
Said Suit shall be in a sum not to exceed Six Thousand
($6,000 ) Dollars .
The Attorney General of the Commonwealth of Kentucky
may investigate the claim against the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky herein , and settle said claim before or after suit is
filed , if he deems it advisable . Any amount recovered in said
suit or by settlement with the Attorney General shall be paid
out of the State Road Fund upon warrant drawn by the
Commissioner of Finance upon the State Treasurer .
In the event suit is filed and judgment rendered by the
circuit court, either party may appeal to the Court of Appeals
of Kentucky .
No statement in this resolution shall be considered by
either the court or the jury as an admission of negligence
upon the part of the Commonwealth and this resolution shall
not be introduced as evidence , nor commented upon by counsel
in the argument of the case to the jury .
Not approved .
Received by Secretary of State March 30 , 1948 ,

CHAPTER 259 597
to the contractor, and for which the contractor was never
compensated ; and
WHEREAS , a dispute has arisen between said contractor
and the Commonwealth of Kentucky with respect to additional
compensation which the contractor claims to be due him
because of various and sundry requirements imposed upon
the contract which were not contemplated in or required by
said contracts and specifications ;
NOW , THEREFORE , in order that the respective rights
of the parties may be finally determined , the said E. H. Mar-
hoefer , Jr. Company hereby is authorized to institute and
maintain a suit against the Commonwealth of Kentucky , at
the contractor's election , either in the Christian Circuit Court
or the Franklin Circuit Court , for such additional compensa-
tion as it may be entitled to receive and for such damages
as it may have sustained , if any . The Attorney General of
the Commonwealth of Kentucky may investigate the said
claims against the Commonwealth and may compromise or
settle said claims before or after suit is filed if he deems it
advisable . Any amount adjudged to plaintiff in said suit or
agreed upon by settlement with the Attorney General shall
be paid out of funds appropriated to the Department of Wel-
fare or out of any other available funds of the Common-
wealth , upon warrant drawn by the Commissioner of Finance
upon the State Treasurer . In the event suit is filed and judg-
ment rendered by the circuit court, either party may appeal
to the Court of Appeals of Kentucky , as in other civil cases .
Nothing contained in this Resolution shall be considered
by either the court or jury as substantive evidence on the
merits of the alleged claim , and the court shall instruct the
jury that the Resolution authorizing this suit shall not be
considered as an admission by the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky or any of its agencies of any liability on the part of
the Commonwealth of Kentucky or any of its agencies , nor
an admission that any verdict of any kind whatsoever shall
be rendered for the plaintiff .
Not approved .
Received by Secretary of State March 30 , 1948 .

CHAPTER 264 601
WHEREAS , it was an ambush slaying and a case of purely circum-
stantial evidence requiring emergency action , which the Common-

and
WHEREAS , the Game and Fish Commission employed the law firm of
Rodes K. Myers , of Bowling Green , Kentucky , to investigate and

WHEREAS , the law firm of Rodes K. Myers relied upon said contract
and entered into said duties prescribed by said contract and has

prosecution , and
WHEREAS , said contract provided for the payment of a fee of twenty-
five hundred dollars ( $2500.00 ) , and
WHEREAS , the Attorney General has ruled that the Game and Fish
Commission must have authority by law to pay said fee ,
NOW, THEREFORE ,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky that,
Upon requisition of the Division of Game and Fish , the
Commissioner of Finance hereby is directed to issue a war-
rant in the sum of twenty -five hundred dollars ($ 2500.00 )
payable to the law firm of Rodes K. Myers , and the State
Treasurer is directed to honor and pay said warrant upon
its presentation . Such payment shall be charged to the funds
of the Division of Game and Fish .
Not approved .
