CHAPTER 265 603
CHAPTER 265
(H. R. 64 )
A JOINT RESOLUTION authorizing Mrs. Martha Howard and Ella
Howard , of Magoffin County , Kentucky , to sue the Commonwealth
of Kentucky and the Department of Highways , or either .
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
That Mrs. Martha Howard and Ella Howard are each
authorized to sue the Commonwealth of Kentucky and the
Department of Highways of Kentucky , or either , in the
Magoffin Circuit Court , for damages resulting from personal
injuries and medical expenses , caused by alleged negligence
on the part of an employe of the Department of Highways
and the Commonwealth of Kentucky , while said employe was
in the line of duty.
The suit of the said Mrs. Martha Howard shall be for a
sum not in excess of $7,000 , and the suit of the said Ella
Howard shall be for a sum not in excess of $7,000 .
The Attorney General of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky may investigate the claims herein and settle same be-
fore or after suit is filed , if he deems it advisable .
Any amount recovered in said suits or by settlement
with the Attorney General shall be paid out of the State Road
Fund upon warrant drawn by the Commissioner of Finance
upon the State Treasurer .
In the event suit is filed and judgment rendered by the
Circuit Court , either party may appeal to the Court of Ap-
peals of Kentucky.
No statement in this Resolution shall be considered by
either the Court or the jury as an admission of negligence
upon the part of the Commonwealth , and this Resolution
shall not be introduced as evidence for either party nor com-
mented upon by counsel in argument to the jury .
Not approved .
Received by Secretary of State March 30 , 1948

CHAPTER 266
(H. R. 70 )
A JOINT RESOLUTION providing for the payment of compensation
for services rendered and expenses incurred in connection with and
related to the present session of the General Assembly ; appropriat-
ing money from the Legislative Session Fund for the purposes of
such compensation ; providing for the distribution of the money so
appropriated ; and declaring an emergency.
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
1. There is appropriated out of the Legislative Session
Fund a sufficient sum of money to enable the payment of
extra compensation , at the rate of $2 per day each , during
the period of the present session of the General Assembly ,
to Noel Jones , custodian ; James B. West , mail operator ;
Robert Crews , utility ; Mrs. Ottie Scott, matron ; Kelly Gil-
bert , elevator operator ; Adrian Sluscher , elevator operator ;
James Weber , guard ; Sam Penn , electrician ; Ernest Wright ,
mimeographer ; Nancy Childs , Dorothy D. Lea , Susan Black
and Irene Crumbaugh , telephone operators ; and Stanley
Berry, mail carrier ; for services beyond the line of duty, in
the operation and maintenance of the State Capitol and its
various facilities , made necessary by reason of the presence
of an abnormal number of persons in the State Capitol , and
at abnormal hours , during the present legislative session .
2. The Chief Clerk of the House of Representatives
shall requisition , and the Commissioner of Finance shall draw
his warrant upon the State Treasurer in favor of said Chief
Clerk for , the sum hereby appropriated . The Chief Clerk
shall forthwith distribute said sum in the manner contem-
plated by this Resolution .
3. Whereas , the General Assembly will within a short
time adjourn and the officers and employes will depart for
various sections of the state , an emergency is declared to
exist and this Resolution shall be effective from and after its
passage and approval by the Governor .
Not approved .
Received by Secretary of State March 30 , 1948 .

CHAPTER 268 605
CHAPTER 267
(S. R. 5)
A CONCURRENT RESOLUTION providing for payment of the per diem
of the late J. T. Linton.
WHEREAS , the late J. T. Linton was elected to the House of Representa-
tives of this General Assembly at the general election in November
1945 , from the Logan County Legislative District , and
WHEREAS , the deceased spent considerable time and money in his cam-
paign for nomination and election , and
WHEREAS , it has heretofore been the custom and precedent of the
General Assembly to allow the widow or dependents of deceased
members the per diem they would have received , and
WHEREAS , if the late Representative J. T. Linton had been permitted
to serve in the 1946 Session of the General Assembly , to which he
was duly and legally elected , he would have received as per diem
the sum of $ 15.00 for each legislative day of the Session as a member
thereof , now therefore
Be it Resolved by the Senate of the General Assembly of
Commonwealth of Kentucky , the House of Representatives
Concurring Therein :
That there is hereby appropriated out of the Legislative
Session Fund an amount equal to $15.00 for each day of the
present session of the General Assembly, to be paid to Mrs.
J. T. Linton , widow of the late Representative J. T. Linton
of Logan County, being the per diem the late Representative
Linton would have received had he lived to serve during this
session to which he was duly elected . The Clerk of the House
shall certify the amount specified herein to the Commissioner
of Finance , who is hereby directed to draw his warrant upon
the State Treasurer in the amount specified herein . All laws
or parts of laws , in conflict herewith, are hereby repealed
to the extent of such conflict .
Approved March 22 , 1948 .

It also states that the resolution itself cannot be used as an admission of negligence or introduced as evidence.

610 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Stratton , and Henry Stratton , are each authorized , individ-
ually , to sue the Commonwealth of Kentucky and the Depart-
ment of Highways of Kentucky , in the Floyd Circuit Court ,
for damages resulting from personal injuries caused by
alleged negligence on the part of employes of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky and the Department of Highways , while
said employes were engaged in the line of duty as employes
of this Commonwealth and in its Department of Highways .
Said suits shall not be for a sum in excess of the follow-
ing amounts :
Edmund Hoyle $ 3000.00
Mary Hoyle 5000.00
Charles Edward Hoyle 5000.00
Millard Stratton 2500.00
Dorothy E. Stratton 2500.00
Marvin Ray Stratton 2500.00
Henry Stratton 2500.00
No plea of limitation shall be interposed if said suits are
commenced within one year from the effective date of this
Resolution .
The Attorney General may investigate said claims and
settle any or all of said claims before or after suit is filed ,
if he deems it advisable . Any amounts recovered in said suits
or by settlement with the Attorney General shall be paid out
of the State Road Fund upon warrant drawn by the Com-
missioner of Finance upon the State Treasurer .
In the event suit is filed and judgment rendered by the
circuit court , either party may appeal to the Court of Appeals .
No statement in this resolution shall be considered by
either the court or jury as an admission of negligence upon
the part of the Commonwealth or the Department of High-
ways, and this resolution shall not be introduced as evidence
nor commented upon by counsel in argument to the jury .
Not approved .
Rceived by Secretary of State March 30 , 1948 .

CHAPTER 274 611
CHAPTER 273
(S. R. 13 )
A JOINT RESOLUTION authorizing the payment of the claim of G. B.
Taylor, of Greensburg , Kentucky , for traveling expenses incurred
while serving as a member of the County Boundary Commission
established by Chapter 179 of the Acts of 1944 ; and appropriating
the necessary sum to pay the claim .
WHEREAS , G. B. Taylor , of Greensburg , Kentucky , was duly appointed
as a member of the County Boundary Commission created by Chap-
ter 179 of the Acts of 1944 , and while serving as a member of the
Commission incurred traveling expenses in the amount of $47.67, and
WHEREAS , through inadvertence , the claim for such traveling expenses
was not submitted until after the end of the 1945-1946 fiscal year ,
at which time the appropriation for expenses of the Commission had
lapsed, NOW THEREFORE
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
THAT the sum of $47.67 is hereby appropriated out of
the General Expenditure Fund in the State Treasury , to pay
the claim of G. B. Taylor , of Greensburg , Kentucky , for
traveling expenses incurred while serving as a member of the
County Boundary Commission created by Chapter 179 of the
Acts of 1944 , and the Department of Finance shall draw its
warrant for such amount upon the State Treasurer , in favor
of the said G. B. Taylor .
Approved March 26 , 1948 .

A JOINT RESOLUTION authorizing Dan T. Martin to sue the Common-

That Dan T. Martin is authorized to sue the Common-

damages resulting from automobile damage caused by alleged
negligence on the part of the employes of the Department of
Highways of Kentucky while said employes were in the line
of duty as employes of this Commonwealth .

across the said farm of said R. G. Hanner westwardly . The
said overflows are alleged to have been due to the fact that
the opening is not sufficient to permit passage of the waters
from ordinary rainfalls in that vicinity , which resulted in

CHAPTER 277
(S. R. 19 )
A JOINT RESOLUTION authorizing W. B. Tatum to file suit against the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , the Department of Highways and the
Highway Patrol, or any one or more of them .
WHEREAS , On January 16 , 1947 , while W. B. Tatum was operating his
car westwardly on U. S. Highway 60 at a point approximately one
mile East of St. Matthews , Kentucky , in Jefferson County , the car
of the Highway Patrol then and there being operated by Patrolman
John O'Bryan , an employee of the Highway Patrol and the Depart-
ment of Highways and on the business of the said Highway Patrol
and Department of Highways , was then and there operated in such
a negligent and careless manner as to cause it to go from the East
traffic lanes into the West traffic lane , wherein W. B. Tatum was
driving , and run into the car of said W. B. Tatum , thereby damaging
the automobile of W. B. Tatum and severely injuring him.
WHEREAS , his injuries and damages are of an amount greater than
$1,000.00 and , therefore , his claim is not one that can be properly
brought before the Board of Claims as is provided for in KRS
176.290 .

CHAPTER 278 615
NOW, in order that W. B. Tatum may have his day in court to determine
by judicial action the question of negligence, if any, causing said
property damage and injuries aforesaid ,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
That W. B. Tatum , of Louisville , Jefferson County, Ken-
tucky, is authorized to sue the Commonwealth of Kentucky
in the Jefferson Circuit Court for damages not in excess of
$35,000.00 resulting from personal injuries , medical expense ,
lost time and damage to his car caused by alleged negligence
on the part of employees of the Commonwealth of Kentucky
while said employees were in the line of duty as agents of the
Commonwealth .
The Attorney General of the Commonwealth of Kentucky
may investigate the claim herein and settle same before or
after suit is filed if he deems it advisable .
Any amount , recovered in said suit or by settlement with
the Attorney General , shall be paid out of the Road Fund
upon warrant drawn by the Commissioner of Finance upon
the State Treasurer .
In the event suit is filed and judgment rendered by the
Circuit Court , either party may appeal to the Court of Ap-
peals of Kentucky .
The statute of limitations shall not begin to run in favor
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky until this act becomes
effective .
No statement in this resolution shall be considered by
either the Court or jury as an admission of negligence upon
the part of the Commonwealth , and this resolution shall not
be introduced as evidence for either party nor commented
upon by counsel in argument to the jury .
Not approved .
Received by Secretary of State March 30 , 1948 .

manager of the Kentucky State Fair during the period from January

WHEREAS , C. W. Taylor served , in good faith , in the capacity of man-
ager of the Kentucky State Fair , from January 1 , 1947 , to June 1,
1947 , under a bona fide appointment by the State Fair Board ; and
WHEREAS , notwithstanding that his appointment was held to be invalid ,
his service was in the capacity of a de facto officer , such as to entitle
him to compensation ; and
WHEREAS , the Commonwealth received the benefit of the services of
the said C. W. Taylor and therefore is morally obligated to com-
pensate him for such services ;
NOW THEREFORE ,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
That the State Fair Board is authorized and directed
to pay to C. W. Taylor, out of any funds at the disposal of
the Board , the sum of $2,083.30 , as compensation for his
services as de facto manager of the Kentucky State Fair
during the period from January 1, 1947 , to June 1, 1947 .
Not approved .
Received by Secretary of State March 11 , 1948 .

CHAPTER 282
(S. R. 36)
A JOINT RESOLUTION authorizing Harvey D. Brown , of Lexington ,
Kentucky , to sue the Commonwealth of Kentucky and the Depart-
ment of Welfare , or either .
WHEREAS , on the 10th day of October , 1947 , while Harvey D. Brown
was operating his truck , acting by and through his agent and servant ,
William E. Thomas , upon a public highway in Fayette County , Ken-
tucky , a collision occurred between said truck and an automobile
belonging to the Commonwealth of Kentucky and operated and
driven by Cecil Van Hoose, an employee of the Kentucky Houses of
Reform , with the result that Harvey D. Brown's truck was damaged
in the amount of One Thousand ( $ 1,000.00 ) Dollars , and
WHEREAS , it appears that the Commonwealth of Kentucky or the
Department of Welfare , or either , acting by and through their agent ,
servant and employee , who was then and there acting within the
scope of his authority , may be responsible in damages ,
NOW THEREFORE ,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
1. That Harvey D. Brown , of Lexington , Fayette Coun-
ty , Kentucky , is authorized to sue the Commonwealth of
Kentucky and the Department of Welfare , or either , in the
Fayette Circuit Court , for damages to his automobile result-
ing from the collision caused by the negligence on the part
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky and the Department of
Welfare , acting through its agent and employee , while acting
within the scope of his employment as herein set out .
2. The suit shall be for a sum not in excess of One
Thousand ( $1,000.00 ) Dollars .
3. The Attorney General may investigate the claim
herein referred to and settle said claim before or after suit
is filed , if he deems it advisable . Any amount recovered in
said suit or by settlement with the Attorney General shall be
paid out of the General Fund upon warrant drawn by the
Commissioner of Finance upon the State Treasurer .
4. In the event suit is filed and judgment rendered by
the circuit court , either party may appeal to the Court of
Appeals of Kentucky .

Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky : THAT :
Section 1 of Chapter 267 of the Acts of the General As-
sembly for 1942 regular session , be repealed , amended and

the Governor's discretion , and have a certified copy of same ,
or a copy of any renewal of the Compact deposited with the
Department of State of the United States in Washington ,
D. C. , as provided by Article VIII of said Compact .
Approved March 26 , 1948 .

out of the State Treasury for the purpose of paying school teachers '

purposes was increased by 26 % ; and
WHEREAS , during the same period , notwithstanding such increases in
the amount of funds available for the payment of teachers ' salaries ,
the average of such salaries was increased by only 98 % ; and
WHEREAS , a deplorable condition exists with respect to the teaching
profession in Kentucky , in that the salaries paid to teachers in
Kentucky are the lowest or among the lowest in the United States,

entries.ocr_text:
628 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
as a result of which the best qualified teachers are leaving this
state to engage in their profession elsewhere , and capable students
are no longer attracted to enter the teaching profession ; and
WHEREAS , the children of our citizens are being deprived of the
benefits and advantages of instruction by competent and adequately
compensated teachers, and thus are under a handicap in competition
with children of other states ; and
WHEREAS , it appears that injudicious , imprudent or unskillful admin-
istration of school affairs may be responsible , in whole or in part ,
for the failure of the money made available for teachers' salaries
to adequately meet the need; and
WHEREAS , a limited investigation of the management of county school
affairs , conducted in recent years , disclosed substantial evidence of
mismanagement of school finances ; and
WHEREAS , it appears that a thorough and complete investigation , study
and survey of school affairs , at both the local and state levels , may
reveal situations and practices detrimental to the public interest ,
and furnish the basis for remedial legislation or executive action
with a firm factual foundation ; and
WHEREAS , the Legislative Research Commission established by the
present General Assembly is admirably designed to conduct an
investigation , study and survey of school affairs , and to make an
unbiased and comprehensive report thereon ; and
WHEREAS , the present circumstances with respect to school affairs
constitute an emergency justifying the use of every emergency
power of government ;
NOW THEREFORE
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
That the Legislative Research Commission be and it
hereby is authorized and directed to make and conduct a
thorough and complete investigation , study and survey of the
administration of public school affairs in the Commonwealth ,
from both the local and the state aspect , and to submit to the
1950 General Assembly a full and comprehensive report
thereof .
That in conducting such investigation , study and survey
the members and authorized employes and representatives of
the Legislative Research Commission shall have access to the
records , offices and files of the State Department of Educa-
tion and of every school district and state institution of learn-
ing , and shall have power to make such audits and make
photostatic or other copies of such documents , papers or
records as deemed necessary or desirable .

ers by such persons .

No statement in this Resolution shall be considered by

CHAPTER 293
( S. R. 59)
A JOINT RESOLUTION authorizing Charles R. Hagerman , of Ohio
County , of Kentucky , to sue the Commonwealth of Kentucky and
the Department of Highways , or either ,
WHEREAS , on the 13th day of September , 1946 , while Charles R.
Hagerman was operating his vehicle upon a public highway in
Ohio County , Kentucky , at or about two and one -half miles East
of Hartford on the Hartford -Dundee State Highway No. 69 , a colli-
sion occurred between said vehicle and a vehicle belonging to the
Commonwealth of Kentucky and assigned to the Department of
Highways , with the result that Charles R. Hagerman was severely
and permanently injured and his vehicle was damaged , all in the
total amount of Sixteen Thousand ( $ 16,000.00 ) Dollars , and
WHEREAS , it appears that the Commonwealth of Kentucky and the
Department of Highways , or either , acting by and through their
agent , servant and employee, who was then and there acting within
the scope of his authority , may be responsible for damages ,
NOW, THEREFORE ,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
1. That Charles R. Hagerman , of Ohio County, Ken-
tucky , is authorized to sue the Commonwealth of Kentucky
and the Department of Highways , or either , in the Ohio Cir-
cuit Court for damages to his person and to his vehicle
resulting from the collision caused by the negligence on the
part of the Commonwealth of Kentucky and the Department
of Highways , or either , acting through its agent , servant and
employee , who was then and there acting within the scope of
his authority , as herein set out .
2. The suit shall be for a sum not in excess of Sixteen
Thousand ( $ 16,000.00 ) Dollars .

WHEREAS , the Commissioner of Finance paid to her only the sum of
$390.00 for the services she performed as Chief Clerk of the Second
Extraordinary Session of 1944 , which amounted to a deficit of $ 390.00
of the amount provided for by the Constitution of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky , and
WHEREAS , for said services in both Sessions as Assistant Clerk and as
Chief Clerk she was entitled to receive the total sum of $ 1,260.00
for services performed in proofreading the Journals of the Senate
as provided by the Constitution and was paid only the total sum of
$630.00 , and there remains owing to her by the Commonwealth of
Kentucky a balance of $630.00 ,
NOW, THEREFORE ,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
That the Commonwealth of Kentucky is indebted to Miss
Mary Lou Hubbard in the sum of $630.00 and
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that the Commissioner
of Finance is hereby directed to issue his warrant in the sum
